F001/F003 establish that any fact the optimizer assumes must be checked — there is no third state between checked-redundant and unsound-assumed (round2-synthesis VERIFICATION SPINE). The synthesis already mandates checkable redundancy channels because same-pass writer output is error-correlated with itself, and the syntax verdict makes writer-stated inferable facts cross-checked with mismatch = hard error. Full annotation makes that checksum channel total rather than optional: every interior fact is a second derivation the checker must reconcile. D2 plausibly INCREASES this channel's value: a model writing a language it knows only from in-context spec has no training prior, so hallucination rates are plausibly higher exactly where the drift-checking redundancy earns its tokens (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED both halves; this is prosecutor COUNT 2 of round2-type-inference, still unresolved — my position is the regime that keeps the channel alive while the flagship experiment runs). F009 supplies the architectural precedent: performance-critical semantics visible in the artifact tools consume, never buried as implicit effects — the writer's source is the artifact the verifier and the next model both consume.

THE TOKEN POLICY (concrete, three tiers, all factoring, no inference). T1 — vocabulary: every fact atom short and canonical, one spelling, no long form (so no alternate-spelling violation); numeric modes FUSED into operator atoms (e.g. one atom meaning 'add-wrapping-32'), which satisfies the decided per-node-mode floor (N002/N001/D010) literally at near-zero marginal tokens. T2 — factoring by declaration: named effect-row aliases and Ada-style named instantiation declarations (already anticipated as the naming machinery in round2-type-inference) plus contract-group references (the kernel round2-interfaces/synthesis already requires): declare once at unit head, reference by short name everywhere; expansion is single-lookup substitution, checked, never inferred. This is what flattens the structurally-sound O(k^2) instantiated-type blowup to O(k) declarations + O(1) references. T3 (contested tier, severable): one declared default per fact dimension per unit head for INTERIOR sites only — boundaries always spell their facts per the decided floor — with a bare-mandatory canonicality rule (a site equal to the declared default MUST be bare; different MUST annotate), so exactly one legal spelling exists per program point and expansion is a trivially bijective single substitution, discharging the codec obligations by construction. Consistency-layer consequence priced honestly: content-addressed dedup must hash post-expansion trees, since byte identity across units no longer implies fact identity. Budget accounting: annotation overhead (fact tokens / program tokens) is measured with real BPE tokenizers in CI as a first-class metric per D2; spec-side, the vocabulary is a table — the cheapest spec shape to teach (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED).

WHY NOT COMPILER-CARRIED SPARSE SOURCE: it forfeits the interior redundancy channel entirely (only one derivation exists at elided sites), reopens the codec/second-spelling situation the syntax verdict polices, splits the source of record in repair loops, and makes the model bilingual in its own language under a finite D2 budget — all to save a write-side token delta that the mandatory elaborated re-read largely repays. My position keeps every decided floor intact, requires reopening nothing, and degrades gracefully: if T3's defaults are ruled too close to the codec line, T1+T2 alone still deliver most of the compression with zero context-sensitivity.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Verified: safe Rust's exclusivity makes overlapping mutation impossible — the corpus's exemplar that writer-carried, checker-verified facts in the type system are the sound channel; also (negative scope) unchecked writer promises depend on truthfulness, so annotation density is only safe because everything is checked, which my position preserves.

Verified: LLVM noalias facts are UB-if-false promises — the reason there is no free third state between checked-redundant and unsound-assumed; underwrites the argument that elided facts lose the redundancy channel while stated facts must be (and are) cross-checked.

Verified: SPARK ships declared, toolchain-checked restrictions (anti-aliasing, ownership, side-effect-free expressions) — precedent that a verification-first language puts explicit declared contracts in the writer's source and restricts surface expressiveness rather than inferring intent.

Verified: Swift SIL exposes ARC operations explicitly instead of hiding them — the architectural lesson that performance-critical semantics belong visibly in the artifact tools consume; supports facts-in-the-written-source over facts-materialized-only-in-derived-artifacts.

Direct-extraction (per round2-type-inference): numeric semantics are per-operation optimizer contracts, grounding the decided per-node mode-naming floor; my fused operator-mode atoms satisfy that floor literally at minimal token cost.

Direct-extraction: implicit conversions silently reassign per-operation optimizer contracts — why compression must be factoring (declared, single-lookup) and never inference or implicit variation; my 'zero implicit variation' clause is this card's design consequence.

Direct-extraction: conversions perturb dispatch/specialization selection — reinforces that any bare-site resolution must be a deterministic single lookup from an explicit declaration, never context-sensitive inference.

CONFIRMED 2026-07-02 (card-verification jsonl): monomorphized generics pay no runtime cost — the mono-only, explicit-instantiation floor whose instantiated-type spellings create the O(k^2) token risk my T2 named-instantiation declarations exist to flatten.

CONFIRMED_WITH_AMENDED_SOURCES 2026-07-02: data-race freedom via ownership/Send-Sync-class capabilities — these capability predicates are part of the mandatory annotation vocabulary under D1, so the vocabulary must be taught in-context under ANY regime; the marginal spec cost of full annotation is therefore small.

Decided floor: one canonical spelling, fully elaborated stored artifact, writer-stated inferable facts cross-checked with mismatch=hard error, and the explicit obligation that the canonical grammar 'minimize gratuitous verbosity without adding alternate spellings (short fixed operator atoms)' — my position is the systematic execution of that obligation across the fact vocabulary; its toolchain-boundary policing is also what prices the rival elaborator-as-codec.

Names the open interior-annotation question, the decided boundary-explicitness and zero-implicit-conversion floors, the structurally-sound O(k^2) objection, the Ada-style nominal-instantiation naming machinery, the mandatory elaborated re-read artifact under elision, and the warning that annotation-vocabulary churn risk scales with annotation density — my position's frame, mitigation, and honest cost all come from this file.

Supplies the elaborator-as-codec open tension my position dissolves (~line 113), the mandatory-elaborated-re-read addition (line 67), the checkable-redundancy-channels addition (line 68), the contract-group kernel and consistency layer my T2/T3 build on, and contract-edit gating (line 71) reused for default-declaration edits.

Grants compiler-GENERATED token-compact interface views with zero semantic weight — the precedent that the compiler's role in the token budget is derived READ compression (omitting bodies, never facts), which my position keeps as complementary rather than letting derived artifacts become the semantic record.

Requires writer-stated, compiler-checked lifetime contracts on every allocation with compiler-enforced canonical annotation spellings — an already-decided instance of writer-carried, canonically-spelled annotation density that my policy generalizes; its remanded writer-written-free question shows the court already treats writer-stated redundancy as a live, valuable channel pending experiment.

Ownership facts are signature-carried types (the P3 separate-compilation strawman ruling) — boundary annotations are load-bearing facts, not decoration; and its evidence-gap list (no cards on annotation error rates) is the origin of the EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED discipline every model-behavior claim below observes.

concessions: The corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence cards; every model-behavior claim in this case is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED under the standing rule, including the claims that favor me (uniform rules easier to follow in-context, redundancy more valuable for in-context-taught languages, tables cheap to teach).

The O(k^2) instantiated-type token blowup for deep generic pipelines is structurally sound (round2-type-inference concedes this); my T2 named-instantiation mitigation is a real cost (new naming machinery, more declarations) and its practical adequacy depends on unmeasured code-shape frequency.

Full annotation maximizes the count of wrong-but-well-typed annotation sites — round2-type-inference's own finding that annotation-side drift (let x: u8 = 255-class silent semantic decisions) is a live channel; my density makes that surface as large as it can be.

If the flagship redundancy-independence experiment shows same-pass interior annotations are correlated noise (defender horn b), the drift-checking rationale for interior density collapses and interior annotation reduces to pure token cost; I would retreat to boundaries-dense + T3 default-factored interiors, which is materially closer to my opponent's regime.

T3 defaults-by-declaration sits on the codec continuum the syntax verdict polices: bare sites are context-sensitive within a unit, fragment-level reads need the unit head or an expanded view, and content-addressed dedup must key on post-expansion trees, weakening pure byte-level drift detection across units. If the syntax verdict's one-spelling rule is read maximally strictly, T3 should be dropped; my position is severable and survives on T1+T2.

Annotation-vocabulary churn is a systemic rewrite risk that scales with annotation density (round2-type-inference What-must-be-true) — my regime raises the stakes of vocabulary instability, so mode/effect/capability vocabularies must be frozen before mass generation, and D2 makes late renames doubly expensive (spec re-teach + program rewrite).

The per-node numeric-mode floor is satisfied by my fused operator-mode atoms only under the reading that a fused atom 'names' the mode; if the court requires standalone per-node mode tokens, my numeric token costs rise and T1's biggest saving shrinks.

My spec-budget advantage is not free: bare-mandatory and alias-mandatory canonicality rules are additional spec rules and additional hard-error classes, each costing one-time spec tokens and per-violation repair iterations; I claim the net is smaller than teaching a synthesis algorithm plus two artifact forms, but that net is unmeasured.

Short atoms are only cheap if real tokenizers merge them; the round2-syntax token-cost evidence is a regex proxy on fragments, and a novel language's atoms may fragment into multiple BPE tokens, eroding T1's savings.
risks: Silent wrong-atom drift: with short atoms in the same syntactic slot (e.g., wrapping vs trapping add), a one-character hallucination that still typechecks is a silent semantics change; D2 plausibly worsens this because an in-context-taught model has no trained prior separating the atoms. Mitigation (edit-distance-separated vocabulary design, cross-check against inference where inference exists) is partial — where no second derivation exists, nothing catches it.

Default-edit blast radius (T3): one token changed in a unit head silently changes the governing fact of every bare site in the unit — a concentrated action-at-a-distance channel. Mitigated by making default edits gated contract-edits plus compiler-emitted site-enumerated change lists, but the residual risk is exactly the high-leverage single point the canonical-form philosophy elsewhere avoids.

Density times churn: if any fact vocabulary changes after mass generation, my regime triggers corpus-wide rewrites proportional to annotation density — the largest possible rewrite events of any regime on the table.

Tokenizer dependence: the entire T1 economy assumes short canonical atoms cost ~1 token under the deployed models' tokenizers; if they fragment, full-annotation-compressed converges toward full-annotation-verbose, the exact regime D2 amended away.

Repair-loop friction at scale: uniform density multiplies trivial-mismatch diagnostics (round2-type-inference's friction-error class); if iterations-to-green degrade materially versus elided interiors, the compile-loop economics that justify canonical form start paying against me.

Boundary creep: T2/T3 machinery (row aliases, named instantiations, unit-head defaults) is new core surface that must itself be specified, checked, and taught; each addition spends the same finite D2 spec budget my position claims to conserve, and an accretion of micro-rules could end up costing more spec tokens than one clean synthesis algorithm.
empiricalUntested: Read:write token ratio in AI repair loops (decides how much elision actually saves given the mandatory elaborated re-read artifact). Experiment E1: instrument matched read-modify-write tasks, count tokens read vs written per regime, extending round2-type-inference's token-economics follow-up with D2 accounting.

Uniform-density vs conditional-elision rule-following by models taught a novel language purely in-context. Experiment E2: same kernel spec taught two ways (full-annotation rule vs synthesis-algorithm + elision rules); measure spec token cost with real tokenizers, first-check success, and repair iterations to green.

Short-atom confusability: silent wrong-mode/wrong-capability rate for short canonical atoms vs word-length atoms, with and without in-context-only exposure. Experiment E3: differential testing against reference semantics on generated corpora; measures the wrong-but-well-typed rate my density maximizes.

Redundancy value under D2: whether checked interior redundancy catches MORE writer errors when the language is in-context-taught (no training prior) than in-distribution. Experiment E5: re-run the flagship redundancy-independence experiment (round2-type-inference follow-up 1) with language familiarity as a controlled variable, measuring catch rate vs correlated-wrongness rate.
Defaults blast radius and repairability (T3): injected wrong-default and wrong-override edits; detection and repair rates with gated edits plus site-enumerated diffs vs without. Experiment E4.

T2 adequacy against O(k^2): whether named instantiation declarations flatten instantiated-type token growth on deep generic pipeline shapes in practice, and at what naming-machinery overhead. Experiment E7, riding the flagship drift experiment's template arm.

Real-BPE whole-program token costs of compressed-canonical vs verbose-canonical vs sparse+elaborator regimes (the round2-syntax A/B extended to annotation density, not just infix vs prefix). Experiment E6.

Whether models emit and respect unit-head declarations (defaults, row aliases) reliably enough that factoring doesn't just relocate hallucination from sites to heads — no card, no proxy exists. Folded into E2/E4.
The premise that a tabular annotation vocabulary is cheaper to teach in-context than a procedural synthesis spec — plausible, uncarded; measured as the spec-token and comprehension-error arms of E2.

result.position: Dual-representation: the stored, checked, single source-of-record form stays the alien, byte-canonical, fully-elaborated artifact exactly as round-2 law requires; teachability under D2 is purchased entirely at a separate, version-pinned, bijective familiarity codec (Rust/C-adjacent notation) that the model writes and reads, which the toolchain immediately canonicalizes, never stores, and polices with parse-print-parse fixpoint proofs and round-trip differential fuzzing. The second-spelling risk round 2 warned about is accepted as a transit-only spelling and managed by bijection proofs plus technical storage policing.
result.coreArgument:
1. THIS IS THE PRE-AUTHORIZED PATH, NOT A REOPENING. The round-2 syntax verdict cut human syntax from the core but explicitly held the door open for exactly this design: the write-path codec is "additive tooling over a fixed canonical core — a bijective renderer can be admitted later behind the defender's own A/B experiment without reopening the core decision" (round2-syntax.md, Rationale), and its admission conditions are already written into law: bijective, version-pinned, parse-print-parse fixpoint invariant, canonical-only storage, gated on the A/B experiment (round2-syntax.md What-must-be-true #8; round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md line 75). Of the three positions in this debate, dual-representation is the ONLY one that satisfies D2 without touching a decided floor: pure-alien simply eats D2's cost with no hedge, and familiar-as-stored-form must overturn the canonical-form floor itself (byte-level uniqueness and tree-isomorphism are structurally hostile to C-family notation with its formatting and parenthesization freedom — you end up with a 'canonical Rust-like' that is neither familiar nor regular, and puts precedence resolution inside the trust path with no quarantine layer). D2 is precisely the "cause" that promotes the parked codec from someday to now: round 2 parked it because verbosity was free; D2 revokes that premise by directive.

2. D2 REPRICES THE ONLY MEASURED COST IN THE ENTIRE SYNTAX DEBATE. The sole quantitative artifact is the syntax hearing's token proxy: canonical S-expression forms cost 1.35–1.48x proxy tokens and 1.93x characters versus infix on expression-dense kernel fragments (round2-syntax.md, AI-writer considerations). Under round-2's "verbosity is free," that was noise. Under D2's PROGRAM budget it is a per-code-unit tax on a finite shared context window, paid on every generation, every re-read, and every repair iteration — and the spec sharing that same window makes the tax compounding. The codec refunds the multiplier while the stored artifact is byte-for-byte unchanged, so every canonical-form benefit survives untouched: content-addressed subtree hashing, drift detection, node-path diagnostics, iteration-loop caching, and all four mechanisms of the unified consistency layer (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md lines 22, 54, 66-70) operate on canonical bytes that the codec never modifies. On the SPEC budget, the codec spec can be written as a diff against the model's prior ("C precedence; Rust-like &/&mut; except...") whereas an alien grammar's spec is 100% novel productions. The prior-import discount is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED — but the rival claim, that models absorb an alien regular grammar from a few in-context examples, is exactly as untested (round2-syntax.md logs the training-distribution penalty as "admissible, unquantified"), and only one of the two bets carries a hedge: a codec is droppable tooling if it fails its gate; an alien-only or familiar-only stored surface is a permanent commitment.

3. THE VERIFICATION CASE THAT KILLED HUMAN SYNTAX DOES NOT APPLY TO A BIJECTION. Round 2's kill was verification-driven: desugaring and elision are unverified frontend transformation surface at the soundness chokepoint (F003 negative scope; misleading-comparisons #5), every rewrite priced at Alive2 proof-grade cost (N006), and elision destroys contract-bearing facts (N001/N002 modes, F001/F003 ownership). A bijective codec does none of these things. It is a notation isomorphism on the SAME checked tree: no inference, no elision, no semantic choice — ownership capabilities, effect rows, numeric modes, and explicit conversions render fully and explicitly in BOTH notations (non-elision is a codec design constraint, not an option). Its correctness burden is one global, mechanically fuzzable invariant (parse-print-parse fixpoint plus differential round-trip testing), not N per-sugar-rule semantic-preservation proofs. And it sits OUTSIDE the trust path: the compiler ingests only canonical bytes; a codec defect manifests as a parse failure or fixpoint mismatch — a build error — never as a wrong fact reaching the verifier spine, which already rejects facts not stated-and-checked or checker-derived (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md line 33). F006's SPARK lesson — restrict surface expressiveness to keep the source-to-logic mapping simple — is honored where it binds: the checked artifact.

4. THE ARCHITECTURE ALREADY OWES US THIS MACHINERY. The synthesis independently mandates compiler-generated token-compact interface views, never stored (line 59), and the compiler-emitted fully-elaborated canonical form as the mandatory re-read artifact (line 67); the type-inference verdict's elision option makes the elaborator itself a codec that "must meet the bijectivity/fixpoint rules" (open tension, line 113). Dual-representation does not add a new kind of thing to the toolchain — it names, unifies, and disciplines a codec layer that three round-2 rulings already require, and closes the line-113 tension (which artifact is source of record during a repair loop: always canonical; codec text is ephemeral transit, parsed-canonicalized-discarded) instead of leaving codec-like machinery to accrete unpoliced.

5. VERSION-PINNING IS THE LONG-RUN ARGUMENT. Training distributions move; today's Rust prior is a 2026 fact about model corpora, not a law of nature. A pinned codec is a swappable adapter between drifting model priors and permanent stored artifacts: codec v2 ships for next year's models without rewriting one stored byte, and old code needs no migration because codec text was never stored. Familiar-as-stored fossilizes a 2026 training distribution into the language forever; pure-alien bets the whole design on an unmeasured in-context learning curve. Dual-representation is the only position whose familiarity bet is reversible at tooling cost rather than language cost.

6. THE SECOND-SPELLING RISK, MANAGED NOT DENIED. The design accepts a second TRANSIT spelling, never a second STORED spelling: repos, diffs, hashes, provenance records, and caches are canonical-only, policed technically (CI rejects non-canonical bytes); diagnostics carry canonical node-paths as the machine-readable payload with codec rendering as annotation; codec print mandates parentheses in mixed-precedence contexts to attack misbinding at print-back time; contract-edit gating and the fact-provenance rule are untouched. One honest amendment is required: the round-2 "echo only canonical form in all compiler output" bullet should be narrowed (cause: D2) to "canonical node-path payload mandatory; codec rendering permitted as annotation" — this touches a policing obligation, not any of the seven decided floors, and the alternative (model must read canonical anyway) merely converts the codec into write-only tooling rather than killing it.

NOTE ON CITATIONS: no round-3 verdict files exist in the corpus (debates/ contains round-1 and round-2 only, verified by directory listing); nothing above relies on round-3 rulings. All load-bearing semantic cards this position leaves untouched (D001, C004, D002/D003/D005/D007, J004, A001/A002/A006) were CONFIRMED in the 2026-07-02 adversarial verification pass — the semantic core is hardening independently of the surface question, which is exactly why the surface bet should be made in a reversible layer.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Pre-authorizes the dual design: the bijective write-path codec is ruled 'additive tooling over a fixed canonical core... admitted later behind the gating A/B experiment without reopening the core decision'; What-must-be-true #8 already specifies the admission contract (bijective, version-pinned, parse-print-parse fixpoint, canonical-only storage). Also supplies the only measured cost in the debate (1.35-1.48x proxy tokens, 1.93x chars for canonical vs infix) and names the two opposing untested error hypotheses (transcription-distance vs precedence-misbinding) the codec gate must measure.

Amends 'verbosity is free' and makes SPEC and PROGRAM token budgets first-class constraints; explicitly delegates 'familiarity leverage vs alien canonical form' to this debate — D2 is the cause that promotes the parked codec to active design without reopening any floor.

Restates as core law that any future write-path codec must be bijective, version-pinned, parse-print-parse-fixpoint-checked, echoing canonical form — the dual design is the instantiation of this clause, and the clause is proof the second-spelling risk was anticipated and given a management regime rather than a ban.

citedEvidence.cardId: round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md lines 59 & 67 (interface views; elaborated re-read artifact)
citedEvidence.howItSupports: The toolchain is already required to generate never-stored token-compact views and a compiler-emitted elaborated re-read form — codec-shaped machinery exists in the design regardless; the familiarity codec unifies and disciplines it rather than adding a new subsystem class.

citedEvidence.cardId: round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md line 113 (open tension: elaborator IS a codec)
citedEvidence.howItSupports: If interior elision wins its experiment, the elaborator already creates the write-path-codec situation with no source-of-record protocol; dual-representation closes this tension by ruling canonical is always source of record and codec/elaborator views are ephemeral transit.

citedEvidence.cardId: F003 (LLVM noalias / alias.scope; UB on violation)
citedEvidence.howItSupports: The frontend-soundness chokepoint argument that killed sugar: facts are sound only if frontend obligations hold. Supports the dual design's load-bearing property — the codec performs no fact transformation, so its failure mode is a parse/fixpoint build error, never an unsound fact emission.

Prices semantic rewrites at proof-grade cost — which is why per-rule sugar died. A bijection is one global round-trip invariant, exhaustively fuzzable and provable once, categorically cheaper than N desugaring proofs; also precedent that optimizer infrastructure operates on single canonical forms, which the stored artifact preserves.

Verification-first precedent for restricting the CHECKED surface; dual-representation honors it exactly where it binds — the checked, stored artifact stays maximally restricted — while familiarity lives outside the checked surface.

citedEvidence.cardId: N001/N002 (LLVM poison/UB ladder; unflagged add vs nuw/nsw as distinct contracts)
citedEvidence.howItSupports: Numeric modes are per-operation optimizer contracts; defines the codec's hard non-elision constraint — modes, ownership capabilities, effects, and explicit conversions must render explicitly in BOTH notations, distinguishing the codec from the cut elision/sugar bundle.

citedEvidence.cardId: F009 (Swift SIL: high-level semantics preserved, ARC visible)
citedEvidence.howItSupports: Performance-critical semantics must stay visible to tools; unaffected under dual-representation because the codec renders the full elaboration — nothing is buried, in either view.
Metadata is sound only if frontend semantics are sound — the reason the codec is architected outside the trust path, ingesting nothing into the compiler except canonical bytes.

citedEvidence.cardId: card-verification-2026-07-02.jsonl (D001, D002, D003, D005, D007, C004, J004, A001, A002, A006 CONFIRMED)
citedEvidence.howItSupports: The load-bearing semantic cards of the round-2 core survived adversarial verification; the semantic core is hardening independent of surface notation, so the surface/teachability bet belongs in a reversible codec layer, not in permanent stored form.

Zero implicit conversions and mandatory explicit boundary facts hold under either surface — the codec renders them, never re-infers them; and its elaboration-record mandate shows the design already accepts multiple machine-generated views over one stored truth.

citedEvidence.cardId: round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md line 96 + missing-research-backlog.jsonl (Syntax A/B, priority high)
citedEvidence.howItSupports: The gating experiment for codec admission is already pre-registered with endpoints (real tokenizers, whole programs, first-parse success, silent-error differential testing, penalty decay with in-context examples); dual-representation extends it rather than inventing an ungated pathway.
Requires citing exact cards and flagging what remains unproven — the discipline applied throughout: every model-behavior claim here is flagged EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED with a named experiment.

concessions: Precedence misbinding re-enters on the write path: a model writing infix under a misremembered precedence table produces a wrong-but-well-formed tree that the codec faithfully canonicalizes — bijection proofs cannot catch a correctly-encoded wrong intent. This is the one silent error class the alien canonical form eliminates by construction (round2-syntax.md hypothesis b), and dual-representation re-admits it; mitigations (mandatory parentheses in mixed-precedence codec print, echo-back diff review) are themselves unproven.

Familiarity imports semantic priors, not just notation: a Rust-adjacent surface invites hallucinated `?` operators, dyn objects, closures, catch/unwind, and implicit-drop-cleanup reliance — constructs this core deliberately lacks (no dyn, no closures, no unwinding, trap=abort, no invisible drop). The false-friend error rate could exceed the alien form's transcription-error rate, in which case the codec fails its own gate and should be dropped.

Dual-representation has the largest total specification surface of the three positions (two grammars plus bijection rules). The bet is that the familiar half is nearly free via prior import and the canonical half is read-mostly or read-never; if the 'like Rust except...' exception list grows long, the spec-budget discount inverts and the false-friend rate rises simultaneously.

The 1.35-1.48x token multiplier motivating the codec is a regex proxy on six expression-dense fragments, not real BPE tokenization of whole programs; the syntax verdict itself notes declaration/control-flow-heavy code inflates less. Real measurement could shrink the PROGRAM-budget case for the codec substantially.

One round-2 obligation must be amended: the 'echo only canonical form in all compiler output' bullet (round2-syntax What-must-be-true #8; synthesis line 75) needs narrowing to 'canonical node-path payload mandatory, codec rendering permitted as annotation' for diagnostics to be codec-readable. This touches round-2 text — an obligation, not one of the seven decided floors — and must be recorded as a D2-caused amendment; the fallback (model reads canonical diagnostics, codec is write-only) weakens but does not kill the position.

Codec engineering, bijection proof infrastructure, and round-trip fuzzing are paid up front, before any evidence exists that models even need familiarity leverage — if in-context learning of the alien form turns out to be fast and cheap, the codec is sunk cost.

No round-3 verdict files exist in the corpus (debates/ contains only round-1 and round-2 files); nothing in this argument can or does lean on round-3 rulings, and the D1-delegated safety-envelope questions (unsafe hatch, runtime-check policy) remain open beside this ruling.
risks: Dialect leakage: codec text gets stored, diffed, or cached by humans or downstream tools and silently becomes a de facto second source of record. Policing must be technical (CI/repo hooks rejecting non-canonical bytes), because round-2's own finding is that passive-benefit obligations do not happen unless mandated — and this risk grows with every tool added to the loop.

Codec proliferation: per-model or per-vendor codecs would recreate exactly the consistency-layer fragmentation the synthesis warns about (line 116, six-subsystems tension). Governance must pin ONE codec per language version; the version-pinning strength becomes a weakness if versioning is used as a dialect factory.

Lenient-input tension: familiarity requires tolerating formatting variance on codec input (models emit flexible whitespace/parens), but every leniency widens the codec grammar and its ambiguity surface; strict input rules erode the very familiarity that justifies the codec. The parse-print-parse fixpoint bounds but does not eliminate this surface.

Repair-loop source-of-record confusion: the model writes codec text, the compiler echoes canonical (or annotated) output, and the model may treat its own stale codec text as ground truth — the synthesis line-113 tension doubled. Requires a hard workflow protocol (codec text discarded after canonicalization; re-reads always from freshly rendered views) that is itself a model-behavior bet.

Gate erosion: under schedule pressure the pre-registered syntax A/B could be skipped or weakened and the codec admitted ungated, converting a managed risk into the unmanaged second-spelling scenario round 2 warned about. Admission must remain procedurally locked to the experiment outcome with pre-committed thresholds.

Bijection scope creep: pressure will mount to make the codec 'slightly nicer' (elide an obvious mode, infer an instantiation) — each such step is exactly the cut sugar/elision re-entering through the codec door. The non-elision constraint (all contract-bearing facts render in both views) must be enforced as a codec invariant checked by the same fixpoint machinery, or the verification case in section 3 collapses.
empiricalUntested: Prior-import discount: a Rust/C-adjacent codec reduces the spec tokens needed to reach a target correctness level versus a self-contained alien grammar spec. EXPERIMENT: spec-budget A/B — fixed total context; full alien-grammar spec vs diff-from-Rust codec spec; measure program correctness per spec token and per total context token.

Training-distribution penalty and decay: models emitting the alien canonical form have higher initial defect rates, and the gap closes at some rate with k in-context examples. EXPERIMENT: the pre-registered syntax A/B decay-curve arm (missing-research-backlog.jsonl, priority high) — real tokenizers, whole programs, defect rate vs example count.

Alien-form transcription errors (round2-syntax hypothesis a): converting infix spec formulas to prefix/canonical form induces silent operand-order/associativity swaps that type-check. EXPERIMENT: differential testing of generated kernels against reference implementations in the A/B's canonical arm.

Codec-side precedence misbinding (round2-syntax hypothesis b), and whether mandatory-parenthesis print-back plus echo-back review catches it: EXPERIMENT: seeded mixed-precedence tasks in the codec arm, differential testing, with and without paren-mandate print-back.

False-friend semantic hallucination: Rust-adjacent notation increases the rate of hallucinated constructs the language lacks (?, dyn, closures, catch/unwind, implicit-drop reliance) relative to an alien surface. EXPERIMENT: seeded-temptation task suite (tasks whose idiomatic Rust solution uses a banned construct) comparing hallucination rates codec vs canonical vs a no-prior control grammar.

Read/write asymmetry: models read alien canonical form accurately and cheaply even when writing it is error-prone or token-expensive, which would let the codec be write-only and void the echo-rule amendment. EXPERIMENT: re-read comprehension and bug-finding accuracy on canonical vs codec-rendered views of identical programs.

Repair-loop coherence: models can act on canonical(-annotated) diagnostics for code they wrote in codec form without ground-truth confusion. EXPERIMENT: multi-iteration repair-loop trial measuring iterations-to-green and stale-view error rate under the transit-only source-of-record protocol.

True program-token multiplier: the 1.35-1.48x canonical-vs-infix cost replicated with real BPE tokenizers on whole programs including declaration-heavy code (the codec's PROGRAM-budget justification). EXPERIMENT: the A/B's whole-program tokenizer measurement, replacing the regex-proxy fragments.

Codec emission reliability: AI writers produce first-parse-valid codec text at higher rates than first-parse-valid canonical text (and reliably emit required provenance records — flagged untested at synthesis line 76). EXPERIMENT: first-parse success and malformed-output taxonomy across both arms of the A/B.

result.position: Signature-dense, interior-light: the writer pays mandatory full annotation at every signature, public type, and instantiation (the round-2 floor, preserved intact); interiors carry minimal mandatory annotation and are checked by deterministic, defaulting-free, ambiguity-rejecting bottom-up type computation; the fully-elaborated artifact exists as a compiler-derived, content-addressed, write-inert view that is retrieved on demand — diagnostics quote elaborated facts at their node-paths — never mandatorily re-read wholesale; mandatory per-program tokens scale with boundary count, not interior expression volume, anchored near hand-written Rust density.
result.coreArgument:
D2 makes every mandatory token a recurring cost paid from a finite shared context budget on every code unit — and, because the language is taught by spec + examples, per-program density multiplies a second time into the one-time SPEC budget through every teaching example. The correct response is to place writer-paid annotation exactly where each token buys a carded optimizer fact or an independent verification check, and to move all derived weight into compiler artifacts that enter context only in slices. Signature-dense, interior-light is the unique regime that does this without touching any round-2 floor.

(1) BOUNDARIES ARE WHERE THE FACTS ARE. Every carded optimizer enabler is a boundary or per-node intent fact: &mut exclusivity and ownership at signatures (F001; C004, now CONFIRMED_WITH_AMENDED_SOURCES 2026-07-02), noalias parameter/return contracts (F003/F004), SPARK's declared subprogram restrictions (F006), named numeric modes per arithmetic node (N002), writer-stated lifetime contracts per allocation (round2-memory-automation). Not one card in F/N/A/D/C/J attributes optimizer or verification value to restating an interior let-binding's type that the checker already uniquely determines. My regime keeps 100% of the carded fact channel at full density and cuts only the uncarded residue.

(2) UNDER THE ROUND-2 FLOORS, INTERIOR 'INFERENCE' IS NOT INFERENCE — IT IS TOTAL COMPUTATION, AND ITS SPEC IS TINY. With mono-only generics and explicit instantiation arguments (D001, CONFIRMED 2026-07-02), zero implicit conversions (N001/N002/D010 floor), and defaulting-free explicit literal types, every leaf of every interior expression has a stated type; interior types are computed bottom-up by a deterministic, non-backtracking, coercion-free function. There is no unification, no solver, no search — the writer can predict what its code means by running the same rule it was taught. D009's silent-degradation exhibit is disarmed exactly as round2-type-inference records: ambiguity is a hard compile error, never a fallback (caveat: D009/D010 remain direct-extraction, absent from the 2026-07-02 verification pass). The in-context spec cost is roughly one paragraph plus examples. The rival regime's spec must ADDITIONALLY teach nominal naming machinery for currently-unnameable types (round2-type-inference 'what must be true' conditions mandatory interiors on exactly this machinery), and its per-program cost is the O(k^2) instantiated-type inflation on deep generic pipelines that the verdict itself rules 'structurally sound.'

(3) D2 CONVERTS ROUND-2'S TIE INTO A PRESUMPTION. Round-2 reached needs_evidence on the interior question under the 'verbosity is free' premise; D2 explicitly amends that premise. The cost side of mandatory interior annotation is now certain, recurring per code unit, and compounding with the canonical form's already-measured 1.35-1.48x proxy inflation (round2-syntax) — multipliers compose on every program forever. The benefit side remains unmeasured and mechanistically suspect: same-pass annotations are error-correlated with the code they annotate via autoregressive conditioning (defender horn b, EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED both directions). When cost is certain-and-recurring and benefit is unproven, the burden of proof flips: interior-light is the presumptive default; the annotation regime must WIN the redundancy-independence experiment outright — showing catch rates that justify measured token and cascade costs — not merely tie.

(4) THE COMPILER CARRIES THE DERIVED WEIGHT, IN ARTIFACTS THAT NEVER ENTER CONTEXT WHOLESALE. The fully-elaborated form still exists, is still mandatory to EMIT, still substrates elaboration-diffing of near-duplicates (synthesis consistency mechanism 4) at zero program-token cost, and still serves re-reads — on demand. This is not a novel architecture; round-2 already sanctioned it twice: compiler-GENERATED token-compact interface views 'on demand, zero semantic weight, never stored' (round2-modules), and compiler-derived drops surfaced as IR ops plus machine-readable per-function lifetime reports while the writer states only the contract (round2-memory-automation) — the memory verdict IS signature-dense/interior-light applied to lifetimes. F009 is the carded precedent: Swift carries derived ARC facts visibly in compiler IR, not in source text. What D2 forces is the precise reading of the synthesis's 'mandatory RE-READ artifact' clause: 'out of contract without the elaborator' must mean the elaborator is mandatory IN THE LOOP — every diagnostic quotes the elaborated facts at its node-path (bounded slice, so the common repair path requires no voluntary retrieval), and the writer can query elaborate(node-path) for any region — not that the inflated artifact is mandatorily ingested on every read. Pull is priced per incident; push is priced per read; under D2 the wholesale reading is self-defeating because it re-spends, on every read, the exact tokens elision saved on every write.

(5) THIS RULING RESOLVES A RECORDED OPEN TENSION INSTEAD OF CREATING ONE. Round2-synthesis logs the unresolved fork: elaborated stored artifact (syntax) vs interior elision (type-inference), with 'neither verdict says which artifact is the source of record during a repair loop.' Ruling: the signature-dense interior-light canonical surface is the single stored spelling and the source of record in every repair loop; the elaborated form is derived, deterministic, content-addressed, and WRITE-INERT — no toolchain path accepts it as program text. Single-spelling law, byte-canonicity, tree-isomorphism, hard-error on non-canonical input: all preserved. No bijective-codec obligation arises because nothing ever writes back from the derived view. This requires explicitly re-opening ONE sub-clause of the canonical-form floor — 'the stored artifact is fully elaborated' — with cause: that sub-clause was priced entirely under the 'verbosity is free' premise that D2, by its own text, amends.

(6) THE PER-PROGRAM TOKEN POLICY, CONCRETELY. MANDATORY (writer-paid): the full intent-fact vocabulary at every signature, public type, and instantiation — ownership/aliasing capability, effect row, numeric modes, ABI-visible layout; lifetime contract per allocation; explicit conversions; explicit literal types; explicit instantiation arguments; exhaustive match arms. These scale with boundary count, not code volume; the anchor is hand-written-Rust signature density (approximate — see concessions). FORBIDDEN AS MANDATE: per-binding interior type/mode/layout — the layout half is already rejected law (F002/F007/J004/F009: interior representation is optimizer-owned), and this position extends the same logic to interior type restatement pending the experiment. OPTIONAL (writer-chosen): interior fact statements are legal anywhere and cross-checked against computation with mismatch as a hard error — the checksum channel survives as a TARGETED spend at drift-prone sites (near-duplicates, elaboration-driving literals) rather than a blanket tax; the spec teaches this as budgeted policy, not style. COMPILER-PAID (zero program tokens): full elaboration, lifetime reports, interface views, elaboration-diffs, site-enumerated edit lists — quoted in diagnostics, retrieved in slices.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Adversarially-verified exemplar that the decisive non-interference facts (safe &mut exclusivity) are signature/boundary-level; the regime keeps this channel at full mandatory density, so no carded optimizer payload is lost by lightening interiors.

CONFIRMED_WITH_AMENDED_SOURCES (2026-07-02): data-race freedom discharged via ownership/Send-Sync-class capability predicates — type- and signature-level facts, not interior let restatements; D1's bar is met entirely from the boundary-dense half of the regime.

UB-if-false alias facts establish the checked-or-unsound dichotomy the type-inference verdict draws from it: interior annotations can only ever be checked-REDUNDANT; their value is the empirical question while their D2 token cost is certain — the asymmetry my burden-flip argument rests on.

SPARK is the shipped precedent for signature-dense/interior-light checking: declared restrictions and contracts at subprogram boundaries, tool-discharged interiors — verification power without per-expression writer restatement.

Swift SIL carries derived facts (explicit ARC ops) visibly in compiler-side IR rather than in source text: the carded precedent that 'visible and checkable' does not entail 'writer-written,' which is exactly the compiler-carries-derived-weight half of this position.

CONFIRMED (2026-07-02): mono-only generics with explicit instantiation make every interior call's types concrete, which is what degenerates interior 'inference' into total bottom-up computation with no solver — the load-bearing mechanism claim of point (2).

Implicit conversions perturb dispatch/specialization; the zero-implicit-conversion floor it motivated is what removes coercion search from interior computation, making elision deterministic. Caveat: direct-extraction, not in the 2026-07-02 verification pass.

Its silent-degradation exhibit convicts only inference-with-dynamic-fallback; ambiguity-as-hard-error disarms it per round2-type-inference's own sub-ruling (b). Caveat: direct-extraction, unverified — this leg must be re-checked when carded.

Numeric modes are per-arithmetic-node named contracts; they stay mandatory under this regime (they are intent facts, not redundancy), keeping interior leaves fully determined and the bottom-up computation total.

With F007/J004 (J004 CONFIRMED 2026-07-02), grounds round-2's rejection of the per-binding layout/mode mandate — the already-decided precedent that interiors belong to the compiler, which this position extends to interior type restatement pending the experiment.

Names boundary-mandatory + deterministic ambiguity-rejecting local synthesis + elaboration records as the leading candidate carried forward; rules the O(k^2) inflation 'structurally sound'; conditions the rival regime on new naming machinery for unnameable types; and defines the redundancy-independence experiment this position accepts as its gate.

Sanctions compiler-GENERATED token-compact interface views 'on demand, zero semantic weight, never stored' — the exact derived-artifact architecture that retrieval-on-demand elaboration generalizes; also supplies the contract-as-second-statement channel that carries the redundancy load at boundaries.

Round-2's own instantiation of this regime: writer states the lifetime contract (boundary), compiler derives drops surfaced as IR ops + machine-readable reports (derived artifact) — proof the weight distribution I propose is already the corpus's ruled pattern for the hardest fact family.

Records the open tension (fully-elaborated stored artifact vs interior elision; source-of-record unassigned in repair loops) that this ruling resolves, and the 'mandatory RE-READ artifact' clause whose wholesale-ingestion reading D2's token math renders self-defeating.

The measured 1.35-1.48x canonical-form proxy inflation is already a per-program multiplier; composing a mandatory-interior multiplier on top is what D2 forbids by default; its single-spelling policing rules define the write-inert obligations my derived elaborated view must satisfy.

Documents that the corpus has no cards on annotation error rates or inference feasibility for internal functions — the gap that makes the interior question empirical — while every sound aliasing road runs through boundary-carried ownership facts, which stay fully annotated here.

D001, C004, J004, D002/D003/D005/D007, A001/A002/A006 survived adversarial refetch — the floors this position builds on are hardening; D009/D010 absent from the pass, so the legs citing them carry explicit caveats.

concessions: The redundancy-independence experiment remains the decisive gate, not this ruling: D2 flips the presumption and raises the rival's bar, but if mandatory interior annotations catch real semantic errors at high uncorrelated rates (prosecutor COUNT 2 vindicated) with low correlated-wrongness, the D1-adjacent safety value outweighs recurring token cost and the interior-light half of this position loses.

Interior-light shifts re-derivation burden onto re-reading models; my mitigation (diagnostics quote elaboration slices; pull-queries) is architecture, not evidence. If models hallucinate interior types on raw-source re-reads instead of retrieving, this regime's end-to-end error rate could exceed the annotated regime's despite the token savings.

'Near hand-written Rust density' is an approximation that likely undershoots: effect rows, numeric modes, per-allocation lifetime contracts, and explicit instantiation arguments push mandatory boundary density above Rust. The defensible claim is boundary-proportional scaling (tokens scale with interface surface, not interior expression count), not Rust-equality.

The storage ruling requires explicitly re-opening one sub-clause of the canonical-form floor ('the stored artifact is fully elaborated'), with D2 as cause. If the court declines to re-open, the fallback — store elaborated, write light — recreates exactly the second-spelling/codec situation round2-syntax polices, and this position then inherits the bijectivity/parse-print-parse-fixpoint obligations it currently avoids.

Elaboration-diffing catches near-duplicate interior drift only post-generation, compiler-side; write-time interior annotations would convert some drift into an error at the drift site immediately. I trade detection immediacy for tokens, and the size of that trade is unmeasured.

Two cited legs (D009 disarming, D010 conversion-perturbation) rest on direct-extraction cards not in the 2026-07-02 adversarial verification pass; per the standing rule and round2-type-inference's own caveat, those sub-arguments must be re-checked when the cards are verified.

Ownership discharge may require interior writer statements at a frontier of unknown size (reborrow/region annotations in Rust practice); if the D1 checker needs interior facts the bottom-up computation cannot derive, mandatory density creeps into interiors and the 'minimal mandatory interior annotation' claim erodes.
risks: Retrieval-discipline failure: if agentic writers do not invoke elaborate(node-path) when uncertain, they reason over silently wrong interior types; forcing elaboration slices into every diagnostic covers the repair path but not exploratory reads of unfamiliar code.

Second-spelling leakage: the derived fully-elaborated view is a standing temptation for tooling to accept as input or for writers to imitate; without hard write-inert enforcement at the toolchain boundary, the syntax verdict's single-spelling law is silently violated.

Ambiguity-error economics: if in-context-taught models cannot predict which elisions the deterministic computation accepts, hard ambiguity errors lengthen repair loops; enough iterations erase the per-program token savings (each loop re-reads context).

Diagnostic-slice token cost is unpriced: a high-defect loop pays per-incident elaboration quotes; in pathological loops the pull model could cost more context than mandatory interior annotation would have.

Annotation-vocabulary churn (round2-type-inference 'what must be true'): concentrating all mandatory weight at signatures makes any change to the ownership/effect/mode vocabulary a rewrite of every boundary in the program corpus and every spec example; density concentration lowers but does not remove this systemic risk.

Spec-budget interaction cuts both ways: the local-synthesis rule plus the optional-checksum policy plus the retrieval protocol are themselves spec tokens; if teaching 'when to optionally annotate' proves harder than teaching 'always annotate', the D2 spec-budget argument weakens.

Load concentration on unverified substrate: like all round-2-adjacent rulings, this one conditions on checked ownership landing substantially as described (round1-aliasing is design_candidate_later, not decided); if the aliasing round is displaced, the boundary fact vocabulary — the thing this regime spends its mandatory tokens on — changes shape.
empiricalUntested: EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: same-pass interior annotations catch AI semantic errors at meaningful uncorrelated rates vs being autoregressively error-correlated noise. Experiment: the round2-type-inference redundancy-independence experiment — matched codegen under mandatory-interior vs elided-interior regimes; measure catch rate of real semantic errors, correlated-wrongness rate, trivial-mismatch friction, iterations-to-green, now logging D2 token budgets per task.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: signature-dense annotation PLACEMENT familiarity from training-distribution languages (Rust-shaped) transfers to a novel language even though the annotation CONTENT (effect rows, modes, capabilities) is alien. Experiment: in-context teaching A/B with matched specs; measure first-parse success, placement-error vs content-error rates separately, and spec tokens to threshold accuracy.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: models re-derive deterministic bottom-up interior types correctly when re-reading raw interior-light source, and invoke on-demand elaboration when uncertain rather than hallucinating. Experiment: re-read error-rate study across raw source vs diagnostic-quoted slices vs full elaborated context (extends the type-inference token-economics follow-up), plus instrumented retrieval-invocation rates in live repair loops.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: the O(k^2) instantiated-type inflation has practical weight in AI-generated code (structurally sound per the verdict, frequency unmeasured). Experiment: code-shape census of deep generic pipelines in generated corpora; measure realized token deltas per regime with real tokenizers.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED (directly measurable): per-program annotation density multiplies into the SPEC budget through teaching examples at the claimed rate. Experiment: render the fixed spec example set under both regimes with real model tokenizers; compare total spec size and downstream first-parse success.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: the ambiguity/hard-error rate of in-context-taught elision and repair-loop length per ambiguity diagnostic. Experiment: seeded program suite; measure elision-failure diagnostics per KLOC and iterations-to-green under the taught synthesis rule.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: diagnostic-quoted elaboration slices suffice for one-hop repair without wholesale artifact ingestion. Experiment: repair-success and latency A/B on identical injected-defect sets — slice-quoting diagnostics vs mandatory full elaborated re-read vs raw-source-only.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: writers use the OPTIONAL interior checksum channel effectively (spending tokens at genuinely drift-prone sites) when taught as budget policy. Experiment: measure optional-annotation placement against sites where drift/defects actually occurred in longitudinal regeneration cycles (piggybacks on the flagship drift experiment).

result.position: Familiarity-first surface: reuse Rust/C-family spellings wherever semantics genuinely match, governed by a loud/silent decision rule — a familiar form is admitted only if every behavioral delta between the model's prior for that form and our semantics is a hard, checker-caught error; wherever a delta can be silent and well-typed, the familiar form must not parse and an alien spelling is mandatory. No round-2 floor needs re-opening: canonical-form-only constrains spelling MULTIPLICITY, not spelling IDENTITY, and familiarity-first is the D2-optimal way to instantiate the lexical identity round 2 deliberately left open.
result.coreArgument:
1. THE FLOORS ARE SEMANTIC; THE DEBATE IS LEXICAL. Every decided floor — canonical form, closed-world, Result+trap=abort, no implicit dispatch, checked ownership, mono-only generics, zero implicit conversions — is either a semantic constraint or a spelling-multiplicity constraint (exactly one spelling, one byte-formatting, grammar isomorphic to the checked tree). None dictates what the single spelling looks like. round2-syntax.md's own 'What must be true' list already obligates the canonical grammar to 'minimize gratuitous verbosity' (short operator atoms, flat n-ary nodes); under D2 that same cost-minimization obligation now extends to the spec-token and program-token budgets, and choosing spellings that sit inside the model prior is the strongest available compression on both. My position therefore requires re-opening nothing; it requires making the still-undecided canonical-grammar choice under D2's amended economics. The opposition's real payload — determinism, one spelling, tree isomorphism, byte-canonicality, content-addressed caching, node-path diagnostics — survives 100% intact under a Rust-shaped canonical grammar, because all of it depends on canonicality, not on alienness. The only thing alienness buys per se is prior-suppression, and my decision rule buys that selectively, exactly where it matters.

2. SPEC-BUDGET ARGUMENT (D2's one-time budget). The spec must teach, in-context, a language whose genuinely novel, D1-load-bearing vocabulary is large: effect rows, ownership/aliasing capabilities, numeric modes, contract groups, provenance records, surfaced drops, site-enumerated edit lists. No model prior covers any of that; teaching it well is where writer soundness behavior is actually made or broken. Every spec token spent re-teaching function-declaration syntax, block structure, match arms, struct/enum forms, and call notation that the Rust/C prior already encodes is a token stolen from the novel vocabulary. Familiar surface lets the spec say 'declarations, control flow, and expressions are Rust-shaped; here is the exhaustive delta list' and spend its budget where D1 lives. Corpus precedent: F006 — SPARK is literally a familiar-surface language (an Ada subset) plus checker-enforced restrictions, and it is the corpus's flagship precedent for verification-first design; 'familiar base + enforced restrictions' is a proven route (for human writers; AI transfer is flagged untested below).

3. PROGRAM-BUDGET ARGUMENT (D2's per-unit budget). The only measured surface-cost datum in the entire corpus points my way: the syntax hearing verified that alien S-expression canonical forms cost 1.35–1.48x proxy tokens and 1.93x characters versus infix on expression-dense fragments (round2-syntax.md, verified in-hearing; script preserved at the scratchpad tokcount.py). Round 2 priced that as acceptable because 'verbosity is free'; D2 explicitly amends that premise. Worse, the cost RECURS: round2-type-inference.md makes the compiler-elaborated fully-explicit artifact the mandatory re-read view for every read-modify-write cycle, so per-unit surface density compounds across every generation and every re-read in every repair loop. Round2-syntax itself pre-registered 'training-distribution penalty for an unfamiliar canonical form is an admissible, unquantified cost' and filed the measure-the-penalty follow-up; D2 elevates that follow-up from optional to gating.

4. THE LOUD/SILENT DECISION RULE — the mechanistic version of 'semantic mismatch is worse than novelty'. Partition every construct: (a) Semantics genuinely match the prior → reuse the spelling exactly. Carded matches: `&`/`&mut` ownership and exclusivity — D1's bar is Rust's own mechanism (F001, C004 CONFIRMED 2026-07-02: '&mut is not allowed to be aliased… impossible to perform a data race'); mono-only generics — D001 (CONFIRMED 2026-07-02: 'performs just as it would if we had duplicated each definition by hand') matches Rust's model exactly, so `<>` generics with mandatory turbofish-style explicit instantiation are semantically honest; Result<T,E>, struct/enum/match/fn/let/braces. (b) Familiar form exists but a prior-vs-semantics delta can be SILENT and well-typed → the familiar form MUST NOT PARSE. Carded cases: bare `+` — N001/N002 establish numeric semantics as per-operation optimizer contracts (wrapping vs nuw/nsw-poison), and the prior's reading of `+` silently selects the wrong contract, the exact silent-reassignment class the zero-implicit-conversion floor exists to kill (D010: conversions perturb dispatch/specialization); so operators are mode-tagged mandatory spellings (`+.wrap`, `+.nsw`-class atoms) and bare `+` is a parse error — familiar glyph, alienized delta. Likewise `|x|` closures (capture inference cut; env-structs are not closures), `dyn` (D003's dyn-compatibility subsystem cut), `throw/catch` (F008 + the two refuted LLVM EH claims; no unwinding semantics), and any spelling that suggests RAII scope-end cleanup, since 'cleanup always runs' is explicitly NOT an invariant. (c) Genuinely novel constructs → novel spellings, full spec budget spent here. D009 (Julia) is the carded cautionary precedent for category (b): familiar, convenient surface hiding semantic divergence produces silent degradation. (d) Deltas that are LOUD — omitted instantiation args, omitted annotations, non-canonical formatting, banned keywords — are cheap: the floors already guarantee they are hard errors, and round 2's machine-diagnostic/edit-list machinery already prices repair loops as an admissible AI cost channel (D0).

5. EXPRESSION GRAMMAR WITHOUT RE-OPENING THE SYNTAX RULING. The verification case against infix was precedence-resolution and desugaring as unverified frontend transformation surface. Mandatory full parenthesization (plus round2-syntax's already-contemplated flat n-ary nodes for associative same-mode operations) has NO precedence table, one derivation, and remains isomorphic to the checked tree — it satisfies the letter and the verification spirit of the ruling while keeping the token vocabulary the prior knows. Consequently I recommend re-scoping, not re-opening: the gated write-path infix-codec experiment (a second-spelling bijective codec the toolchain-boundary policy rightly distrusts) should be superseded by a surface-identity experiment run BEFORE the canonical grammar freezes, choosing the SINGLE canonical spelling with familiarity as a weighted criterion. One spelling, ever; we are only choosing which one.

6. WHY NOT MAXIMAL REGULARITY. The alien-regular position must pay: (i) the full grammar section of the spec from a zero prior, (ii) the measured 1.35–1.48x recurring program-token premium (the only quantified datum either side has), (iii) an unquantified cold-start defect rate that round 2 itself flagged, and (iv) it forfeits nothing I keep — every canonical-form verification benefit is lexical-identity-agnostic. Its one real advantage, wholesale elimination of negative transfer, my decision rule purchases selectively at the sites where transfer is actually dangerous, at the cost of an auditable enumeration obligation (conceded below as my single point of failure).

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Rust &mut exclusivity as carded optimizer enabler — D1's bar is Rust's own mechanism, so reusing Rust's & / &mut borrow spellings is semantically honest reuse, not analogy: the semantics genuinely match, making it the flagship category-(a) familiar form.

CONFIRMED (amended sources, 2026-07-02): data races impossible in safe Rust via &mut non-aliasing. The Send/Sync-class capability kernel the core requires is Rust's own; familiar capability-predicate surface transfers the prior's correct intuitions for the construct whose semantics we adopted wholesale.

CONFIRMED 2026-07-02: Rust monomorphization 'performs just as it would if we had duplicated each definition by hand' — the mono-only generics floor IS Rust's model, so angle-bracket generics with mandatory explicit turbofish-style instantiation reuse a matching prior; the omitted-instantiation habit the prior carries is a loud (type-error) delta, hence admissible under the decision rule.

Poison/UB/undef ordering as explicit optimization contract: numeric semantics are contracts, not conveniences — grounds the rule that bare familiar operators whose prior reading selects a different contract must not parse (silent-delta limb).

Unflagged add is modulo, nuw/nsw make overflow poison — per-operation contract selection means a prior-misread '+' is a silent optimizer-contract reassignment; this is the carded justification for mode-tagged operator spellings replacing bare C operators, i.e., the 'alien where familiar misleads' limb.
Conversions perturb method selection and specialization — supports keeping every representation change an explicit named operation with a spelling the prior cannot mistake for C implicit conversion.

CONFIRMED 2026-07-02: dyn-compatibility is a whole rule subsystem — since it is cut from core, the familiar keyword 'dyn' must be a parse error; familiarity-first mandates ABSENCE of familiar forms whose semantics were cut, preventing prior intrusion at the source.

Julia's familiar/convenient surface hides type-instability and dispatch costs (silent degradation) — the carded cautionary precedent motivating the loud/silent decision rule: familiar surface is only safe where divergence from the prior is checker-caught, never where it degrades silently.

SPARK is an Ada-subset — a familiar surface plus checker-enforced restrictions — and it is the corpus's precedent for verification-first design. Existence proof that 'familiar base + enforced restrictions' is a viable verification-language route (human-writer evidence; AI transfer flagged untested).

EH representation constrains optimization; with unwinding cut and Result+trap=abort law, throw/catch spellings must not exist — another cut-construct-absence case where familiarity-first and the floors agree exactly.

(a) The verdict's floor constrains spelling multiplicity, not identity; (b) the ONLY verified surface-cost datum: alien S-expr forms cost 1.35–1.48x proxy tokens / 1.93x chars vs infix (in-hearing, tokcount.py preserved); (c) it pre-registered 'training-distribution penalty… admissible, unquantified cost' and filed the measure-the-penalty and real-tokenizer A/B follow-ups — D2 elevates both from optional to gating; (d) its 'minimize gratuitous verbosity' obligation is the hook familiarity-first extends to spec/program budgets.

The mandatory compiler-elaborated fully-explicit re-read artifact means surface token density is paid on EVERY read-modify-write loop, compounding the per-unit program-token dividend of a dense familiar surface — and (concession side) means even Rust-shaped code is fully-elaborated and thus partly off-distribution anyway.

Confirms every decided floor is semantic or multiplicity-level, none lexical; its cut list (dyn, closures, catch, implicit conversions, inference) defines exactly the familiar forms that must not parse; its machine-diagnostic and edit-list machinery is what makes loud prior-intrusion deltas cheap under D0's admissible repair-loop cost channel.

D2 names 'familiarity leverage (training-distribution-adjacent surface) vs alien canonical form' as a delegated open question and amends 'verbosity is free' — the direct mandate under which the canonical grammar's lexical identity must now be decided on token economics, where familiarity is the strongest available compressor on both budgets.

concessions: The corpus contains ZERO AI-codegen evidence cards; my central claim — that prior transfer reduces required spec budget and defect rates — is exactly as untested as the opposition's regularity claims. The one quantified datum (1.35–1.48x) is a regex proxy on six expression-dense fragments, not real BPE tokenization of whole programs; declaration-heavy code inflates less, so the measured premium is an upper-bound-flavored estimate on the worst-case slice.

The fully-elaborated stored artifact (every type/mode/effect/contract explicit, explicit drops, effect rows, mode-tagged operators) makes even a Rust-shaped surface substantially off-distribution in practice. The familiarity dividend is plausibly much smaller than surface similarity suggests, and this discount is unmeasured.

A maximally regular alien grammar plausibly has a SMALLER self-contained grammar spec than 'Rust minus a long delta list' — partial familiarity forces the spec to precisely delineate which prior features exist, and the delta list may not be cheaper than a small regular grammar. This is measurable (E1/E3), not assumable, and I do not assume it.

Silent-delta enumeration is my position's single point of failure: one silent delta misclassified as loud (drop timing under Rust-shaped braces is the scariest candidate, since 'cleanup always runs' is explicitly not an invariant) creates a well-typed semantic-drift channel. Maximal regularity eliminates this class wholesale by construction; I manage it per-construct via an audit that can miss.

The prior is a moving target: it drifts across model generations and differs across model families, and Rust itself evolves. A regular alien surface is model-agnostic; mine is anchored to the 2026 C-family prior and carries maintenance/portability cost.

The round-2 verification case for canonical form is conceded in full: no sugar, no precedence tables, no elision, no second spelling, no resugaring — familiarity-first claims only lexical identity within those constraints, and mandatory-parens expression grammar is itself a delta the prior will violate (loud, but at unmeasured repair-loop cost.)

The 'uncanny valley' hypothesis — that partial familiarity is worse than either pole because the model cannot tell which habits apply — cuts specifically against me and, if true at high effect size, flips the verdict to the alien pole.
risks: Silent prior intrusion at a misclassified construct: highest-risk sites are drop/RAII timing (Rust prior deeply assumes scope-end cleanup; the language surfaces drops and does not guarantee cleanup), numeric-mode misreading if any bare-operator form survives review, and snapshot-vs-live expectations around env-structs if any lambda-adjacent spelling leaks in. Failure is well-typed wrong code — the class the charter dreads most.

Repair-loop economics: the prior will emit banned-but-familiar forms (closures, dyn, catch, inferred instantiation, unparenthesized operators) at some rate; all are caught loudly, but if the emission rate is high the iteration-loop cost could exceed the one-time cost of teaching an alien grammar, inverting the D2 calculus.

Spec-by-delta relies on models reliably applying negations ('there is no Drop', 'there is no dyn') against a strong prior — negation-following is a known LLM weak spot; if delta-teaching underperforms self-contained teaching at equal token budgets, the spec-budget argument weakens materially.

Cross-model and cross-generation variance: familiarity benefits and negative-transfer profiles are model-specific; a design tuned to today's prior may misprice both for the writer models actually deployed.

Governance erosion: 'familiar' becomes a standing argument for re-admitting sugar, precedence, or a second spelling over time; the toolchain-boundary policing rules must state explicitly that familiarity never justifies spelling multiplicity, or the syntax ruling un-cuts itself through my door.
empiricalUntested: CLAIM (mine, load-bearing): familiar surface reduces the spec-token budget needed to reach a target defect rate, and reduces cold-start defect rates at any budget. EXPERIMENT E1 — spec-budget sweep A/B: identical semantics rendered as Rust-adjacent vs maximally-regular alien canonical grammars; sweep in-context budget (delta-spec, self-contained spec, +N examples); measure first-parse success, silent-semantic-error rate via differential testing, repair iterations to green, total tokens. Extends round2-syntax's gating A/B, now decision-grade under D2.

CLAIM (opposition's, equally untested): alien regularity eliminates enough silent negative transfer to outweigh its measured token premium. EXPERIMENT E2 — prior-intrusion census: familiar-surface spec with explicit deltas; measure (a) banned-construct emission rate (loud class) and its repair-loop cost, (b) silent-delta error rate (drop-timing assumptions, mode confusion, snapshot-vs-live) via differential/dynamic testing. E2 also directly tests whether my silent-delta enumeration is completable — the position's single point of failure — and the uncanny-valley hypothesis via a partial-familiarity cell.

CLAIM: delta-format specs ('like Rust except NOT X') teach as well as or better than self-contained specs at equal token count. EXPERIMENT E3 — negation-adherence A/B: same semantics, delta-spec vs self-contained spec matched on tokens; measure emission rate of negated features and repair behavior after correction.

CLAIM: the 1.35–1.48x alien token premium holds (or grows) under real BPE tokenizers on whole programs, and Rust-adjacent canonical beats both S-expr and mandatory-parens-infix on real per-unit tokens. EXPERIMENT E4 — the round2-syntax real-tokenizer follow-up, extended to a three-surface comparison including the fully-elaborated re-read artifact rendering (the recurring-cost channel from round2-type-inference).

CLAIM: the training-distribution penalty for alien form decays slowly enough with in-context examples that familiarity still matters at realistic example budgets. EXPERIMENT E5 — penalty-decay curve (already filed as a round2-syntax follow-up): defect-rate gap between surfaces as example count grows; if the gap closes within a small example budget, my position's benefit reduces to token economics only (E4 then decides).

CLAIM: loud deltas are cheap — mandatory parenthesization and mandatory instantiation args cost only bounded extra repair iterations against prior habits. EXPERIMENT E6 — repair-loop pricing of loud prior-intrusion classes under the machine-diagnostic/edit-list toolchain, measured in wall-clock and tokens per green build.

CLAIM: familiarity benefits transfer across model families and survive model-generation drift. EXPERIMENT E7 — repeat E1/E2 across at least two model families and two generations; report variance, pricing the anchoring risk.

result.position: Writer-minimal, artifact-carried: the AI writer emits the least text that uniquely determines semantics (all decided-floor boundary facts included); the compiler deterministically elaborates that into the fully-explicit canonical artifact, which is the ONLY stored spelling and the SOLE read-back source; the in-context spec teaches the minimal writable form as the write skill, with the elaborated form taught only as a slot-filled read superset. Per-program token policy: minimal is the mandated default, optional writer-stated inferable facts remain legal as a cross-checked checksum spend, and redundancy density is a tooling-set per-unit knob tuned by the round-2 experiments, not a spec constant.
result.coreArgument: D2 converts round-2's one open regime question into a forced move, because every token of checker-derivable restatement now displaces spec, examples, or neighboring code from a finite shared context — and round 2 already built my position's machinery under the old 'verbosity is free' premise. Four decided rulings ARE writer-minimal-artifact-carried in miniature: (1) memory-automation — the writer states the allocation's lifetime CONTRACT; the compiler derives drop points and surfaces them as explicit IR ops plus machine-readable lifetime reports (F009 route: Swift writers never write retain/release; the compiler UN-hides them in ITS artifact); (2) generics — the writer names instantiations; the compiler expands bodies (D001, adversarially confirmed 2026-07-02) — the single largest artifact-carried weight in the language, already law; (3) error-handling — context/cause chains are auto-derived with 'zero hand-written tokens per site', which the synthesis calls the move that neutralized the strongest pro-unwinding argument; (4) modules — compiler-GENERATED token-compact interface views with zero semantic weight replace maintained interfaces. My position is not a new bet; it is the generalization of the pattern every round-2 verdict independently converged on, promoted to policy now that D2 prices the alternative. The verification spine is untouched because 'minimal' is defined semantically, not greedily: the decided floors put every intent-bearing fact — ownership/aliasing capability, effect row, numeric mode, ABI-visible layout — mandatorily in writer text at every signature and instantiation (round2-type-inference floors; F001/F006/N002), and those never leave the writable form, because without them semantics is not uniquely determined. What leaves the writable form is exactly the checker-derivable residue: interior types, propagated modes, elaborated instantiation ceremony. For that residue the trust analysis favors the compiler channel outright: F003 says writer promises the optimizer assumes are UB-if-false, so annotations are either checked-redundant or unsound-assumed — no third state — and C004's auto-trait pattern (confirmed 2026-07-02) shows compiler-DERIVED facts are the trusted channel while manual assertion is the UB channel. The round2-type-inference verdict itself rules point (e): under a least-trusted-writer charter, moving derived-fact production into the checker is a verification IMPROVEMENT provided derived facts export into a trusted machine-readable elaborated form — which round 2 separately made mandatory anyway ('any AI workflow reading raw source without the elaborator is out of contract'). So the writer-maximal regime pays for elaboration twice — once in generation, once in the artifact that must exist regardless — while my regime pays once, in the compiler, where D0 says costs are admissible. D2's two budgets both move my way. SPEC budget: teach one write grammar whose elaborated read form is the same tree with slots filled — an incremental read rule, not a second language — and spend the saved tokens on examples, which are the dominant spec cost and inflate 1.35–1.48x under elaborated text on the only measured proxy (round2-syntax, regex proxy caveat). PROGRAM budget: the O(k^2) instantiated-type arithmetic for deep pipelines was already ruled 'structurally sound'; under D2 it stops being a cost and becomes displacement of the very context that teaches the language.
