The writer-error surface also shrinks: full annotation 'maximizes the count of silent semantic decision sites' (wrong-but-well-typed let x: u8 = 255 class, round2-type-inference), and D009-style silent degradation is disarmed because elaboration is deterministic, defaulting-free, and ambiguity-rejecting — inference failure is a hard error, never a silent choice. The second-spelling tension (synthesis open tension: elaborator-as-codec) I resolve rather than deny: the minimal form is a transient WRITE surface, never stored — storage stays single-spelling elaborated, so the canonical-form floor holds; the elaborator/stripper pair is a bijection between the two canonical sets, version-pinned, parse-elaborate-strip fixpoint-checked per the syntax verdict's own codec rules; diagnostics carry dual coordinates (artifact node-path plus provenance-mapped write-surface span); contract-edit gating and content-addressed caching key on ELABORATED bytes so they are regime-invariant. Finally, the policy is a floor, not a ceiling: writer-stated inferable facts stay legal and cross-checked with mismatch as a hard error, preserving the checksum channel; if the redundancy-independence experiment shows same-pass interior annotations genuinely catch errors, the knob turns up at measured-independence sites without changing the language — whereas the writer-maximal regime cannot turn its costs down without a language change. Under D2, uncertainty itself argues for the adjustable-minimal design.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Shipped precedent for artifact-carried weight: Swift SIL un-hides ARC as explicit compiler-placed IR operations that writers never author; round2-memory-automation credits exactly this reading ('visible and checkable' does not entail 'writer-written'). The elaborated artifact carrying compiler-derived explicitness is a proven architecture, not speculation.

Auto-trait pattern (adversarially confirmed 2026-07-02, notes/card-verification-2026-07-02.jsonl): compiler-DERIVED facts are the checker-trusted channel; its negative scope shows manual assertion is the UB channel. Moving derivable-fact production from writer text into the elaborator follows the corpus's only carded trust architecture.

Noalias facts are UB-if-false writer promises, so every writer-authored annotation is either checked-redundant or unsound-assumed (no third state, per round2-type-inference). This caps the verification value of writer-carried redundancy at 'checked-redundant' — a value my regime preserves via the optional cross-checked channel — while its negative scope (frontend emission obligations) honestly names my elaborator-TCB risk.

Monomorphization (confirmed verbatim 2026-07-02): generic bodies are expanded compiler-side, never written per-instantiation. The language's largest annotation weight is ALREADY artifact-carried by decided round-2 law; writer-minimal extends the same principle to interior types and mode propagation.

D1's bar is met by the CHECKER (safe-Rust exclusivity is machine-checked), not by annotation density above the semantic minimum; safety enforcement is indifferent to whether checker-derivable facts are restated in writer text, so writer-minimal does not touch D1.

SPARK's checked boundary contracts justify what STAYS writer-written under my position: declared restrictions at subprogram boundaries, verified every build. This is why 'minimal' includes all intent-bearing signature facts — the round2 floors — and strips only interiors.

Numeric modes are per-operation optimizer contracts, so the ARTIFACT must name a mode on every arithmetic node; but mode intent is stated at boundaries/literals and propagated deterministically — the exemplar of writer-stated intent vs elaborator-carried per-node explicitness.

The silent-degradation exhibit convicts inference with dynamic/heuristic fallback only; round2-type-inference rules that deterministic, defaulting-free, ambiguity-rejecting local synthesis turns elaboration failure into a hard compile error, disarming the strongest anti-elision card.

With F007 (both confirmed): interior representation is analysis-conditional and optimizer-owned; round 2 REJECTED the per-binding layout/mode mandate on these cards — the writer-maximal endpoint is already partially convicted on optimizer grounds, independent of D2.

Compile time is the admissible cost channel my regime spends: content-addressed caching on elaborated canonical bytes keeps the elaborator's added work out of the iteration-loop critical path, per the round-2 additions list.

Halide's algorithm/schedule separation (confirmed 2026-07-02): state intent once, derive the elaborated execution story separately and late — the architectural precedent for keeping writer statements minimal and derivations in a separate compiler-owned layer.

Verdict file: its leading candidate (boundary-mandatory intent-explicit core, interior elision via deterministic synthesis, mandatory compiler-emitted elaboration records) IS this position's mechanism; its floors define my 'semantic minimum'; its follow-ups are my gating experiments.

Verdict file: supplies the codec discipline (bijective, version-pinned, fixpoint-checked, canonical-only storage/echo) under which a minimal write surface is admissible without reopening the canonical-form floor, plus the 1.35–1.48x token proxy and its caveats.

Verdict file: mandates compiler-generated token-compact interface views (zero semantic weight) — artifact-carried compression for the READ path that writer-minimal mirrors on the WRITE path — plus contract-edit gating, which I key on elaborated diffs.

Verdict file: decided architecture is writer-stated contract + compiler-derived, artifact-surfaced drops and lifetime reports; the remanded writer-written-free question maps exactly onto my optional cross-checked redundancy channel.

Names the open tension this position resolves (elaborated-stored-artifact vs interior elision: 'the elaborator then IS a codec'; 'neither verdict says which artifact is the source of record during a repair loop') and already mandates the elaborated form as the re-read artifact plus zero-hand-written-token Result context chains.

Documents that the corpus has NO cards on annotation error rates or inference feasibility for internal functions — grounding the honest status of every writer-behavior claim on both sides as uncarded, which is why my policy ships as an adjustable knob gated by experiments.

concessions: The second-spelling tension is real and my position pays it in full: two canonical forms plus a trusted elaborator/stripper codec, exactly the situation the round2-syntax verdict polices. I accept the entire codec discipline (bijectivity between canonical sets, version pinning, parse-elaborate-strip fixpoint checks, canonical-only echo) as non-negotiable obligations, and I accept that the syntax verdict admitted future write-path codecs only behind a gating experiment — consistency requires my minimal write surface to pass an analogous gate before hardening into law.

The 'halving' headline is an upper bound, not a measurement. True spec saving = (write-rule share removed) minus (elaborated-read-grammar increment + codec-rule spec cost); true program saving = the annotation share of interiors and instantiation ceremony, whose code-shape frequency the round2-type-inference verdict explicitly flags as unmeasured. Boundary facts — which the floors keep writer-written — may dominate token counts in signature-dense code, shrinking my advantage.

If the redundancy-independence experiment shows same-pass interior annotations catch real writer errors at rates justifying their cost (prosecutor COUNT 2), minimal-by-default loses its strongest quantitative claim; my position survives only in amended form (mandatory redundancy at measured-high-independence sites), and the per-program budget rises accordingly.

The elaborator enters the trusted computing base at the corpus's named soundness chokepoint (F003 negative scope; 'unsound frontend metadata emission' in the historical-failure list). My mitigation — elaboration only fills checker-derived slots on the checked tree and rejects ambiguity, rather than rewriting semantics — reduces but does not eliminate this surface, and the checker-implementation-defect gap (round1-aliasing: no cards on verifier defect history) applies to it directly.

The sole-read-back rule means read-back token cost is identical to the writer-maximal regime unless compiler-generated compact views (round2-modules) do real work; if view tooling underdelivers, my regime's net context saving is generation-side only.

A compiler-version change that alters elaboration results is a silent-meaning-change hazard the writer-maximal regime does not have; I must accept version-pinned elaboration rules with surfaced re-elaboration diffs as a hard toolchain obligation, adding to the very toolchain-boundary policing burden round 2 already lists.
risks: Repair-loop coordinate mapping: diagnostics are node-paths into the elaborated artifact, but edits land on the minimal write surface; the mandatory provenance map (artifact node -> write-surface span) is new, untested infrastructure, and any imprecision in it converts machine-actionable diagnostics into mis-targeted edits — attacking the exact property round 2 made a universal contract.

Form-blending in generation: a model that has just read elaborated views may emit elaborated or hybrid text on the write path; non-canonical input is a hard error (friction cost), but near-miss blends that happen to parse are a silent-semantics hazard the single-spelling regime cannot have by construction.

Drift detection becomes post-hoc: in the annotated regime, one-token near-duplicate divergence errors AT the drift site; in my regime elaboration-diffing flags divergence only after the artifact is built — detection rather than prevention, with unmeasured escape rate if diff triage is noisy at AI scale.

Elaborator determinism under a growing fact vocabulary: as ownership, effects, and modes stabilize (round-2 precondition), unique determination from minimal text gets harder; the pressure is either annotation creep back toward elaborated (eroding the token win) or looser synthesis (voiding the D009 disarmament). The design has no third direction if the vocabulary churns.

Contract-edit gating weakens if applied naively: a minimal-source edit regenerates large artifact regions, so gating must diff elaborated contracts semantically, not source text; a bad gate either blocks routine edits (loop cost) or lets a writer launder a hallucinated body through an intent-fact change hidden in a small minimal-source edit.

Governance risk: this ruling resolves the synthesis's second-spelling tension by fiat of one debate; the syntax and type-inference verdicts share custody of that tension, and shipping writer-minimal without formally amending both files reproduces the split-custody incoherence the synthesis warns about (six subsystems, no unifying authorization).
empiricalUntested: EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: models generate minimal-form code with fewer semantic errors and fewer iterations-to-green than elaborated-form code. Experiment: matched codegen tasks, minimal vs fully-elaborated write formats, measuring semantic error rate, first-check pass rate, iterations-to-green, total tokens (extends the round2-type-inference token-economics follow-up).

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: same-pass interior annotations have near-zero checking independence (defender horn b) — the claim that makes writer-carried redundancy a waste of budget. Experiment: the round2-type-inference redundancy-independence experiment, unchanged; it is the single decider for the default knob setting.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: reading elaborated views while writing minimal form does not induce form-blending. Experiment: multi-turn repair-loop sessions with elaborated read-back and minimal write surface; measure non-canonical emission rate, hybrid-form near-miss rate, and repair latency vs a single-spelling control.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: spec tokens saved by teaching only the minimal write form convert into accuracy when reallocated to examples. Experiment: fixed context budget, arm A = minimal grammar + N extra examples, arm B = full elaborated write grammar + fewer examples; measure first-parse and first-typecheck rates (extends the round2-syntax training-distribution-penalty follow-up).

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: the 1.35–1.48x elaborated-text inflation proxy generalizes to real BPE tokenizers on whole programs including annotation-dense signatures. Experiment: the already-gating syntax A/B with real model tokenizers, extended with an elaborated-vs-minimal arm.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: the O(k^2) interior-type inflation is practically heavy in AI-generated code (code-shape frequency unmeasured). Experiment: corpus measurement over synthetic AI-scale programs per the modules compile-loop prototype, counting checker-derivable token share by construct class.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: elaborated-artifact read-back reduces re-derivation hallucination vs reading raw minimal source. Experiment: round2-type-inference's re-read error-rate comparison (raw source vs elaborated views), which my sole-read-back rule presupposes resolving in favor of views.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: provenance-mapped dual-coordinate diagnostics are as machine-repairable as same-surface diagnostics. Experiment: extend the round-2 diagnostics repair-loop asymmetry study with an artifact-node-vs-write-span mapping arm, measuring fix success and latency.

result.position: Repair-loop-primary: ship a deliberately minimal in-context spec (generative kernel + one worked example per core construct); the checker is the teacher. Every rejection carries the missing spec fragment — normative rule text, a counterexample bound to the writer's own code, and a mechanical fix where one exists — so teaching is lazy and paid only for rules actually violated. This requires diagnostics engineered as addressable spec citations and a fast whole-program check loop, with round-2's content-addressed caching promoted from optimization to load-bearing precondition.
result.coreArgument:
1. THE SAFETY ASYMMETRY MAKES LAZY TEACHING FREE OF CORRECTNESS RISK. Under D1 and the round-2 floors, the checker — not the spec — is the enforcement mechanism. A resident spec never guaranteed compliance (a model can misapply a rule it has read); the checker rejects the violation either way. So spec residency is purely a first-attempt-accuracy optimization, and the worst case of an untaught rule is extra repair iterations, never a shipped memory-safety bug or unsound optimizer fact. The debate is therefore about loop economics, not soundness — and F006 (SPARK) is the carded precedent that a verification-first toolchain enforcing declared restrictions, not writer memory, is where correctness lives. F003's lesson points the same way: the hazard class is unchecked writer promises, and no amount of spec tokens converts an unchecked promise into a checked one.

2. LAZY TEACHING CONVERTS A FIXED TAX INTO MARGINAL COST AT MAXIMUM PEDAGOGICAL VALUE. The round-2 language is rule-dense: ownership/aliasing capabilities, effect rows, numeric modes (N001/N002: one mode per arithmetic node), explicit instantiation, env-struct captures, Result-vs-trap classification, lifetime contracts, canonical byte-formatting. A full spec charges every context window for every rule; but any given program exercises a small, task-dependent subset (violation-density distribution: EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED, see census experiment). A diagnostic delivers the rule exactly when it binds: rule text + the model's OWN violating code as the counterexample + the mechanical fix is a worked example generated on demand, centered on the error — the strongest known in-context format, versus unanchored retrieval from a long resident prefix. (That just-in-time fragments beat resident-spec retrieval is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED — the flagship A/B below.) Under D2 the spec competes with the actual optimization task's own context for window space; moving teaching tokens from the prefix into the transcript frees that budget.

3. ROUND 2 ALREADY BUILT THE MACHINE; THIS POSITION ONLY LOADS IT. Six of the eleven 'Additions required because the writer is an AI' in round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md are repair-loop infrastructure: machine-readable node-path diagnostics with cross-file provenance; site-enumerated mechanical edit lists ('a to-do list an unattended writer can execute'); template-level aggregation (one defect, one repair site); the mandatory compiler-elaborated re-read artifact; checkable redundancy channels where mismatch is a hard error (converting hallucination into a diagnostic — i.e., into a teachable moment); content-addressed caching of check results. The syntax verdict (round2-syntax.md) supplies the substrate that makes checker-as-teacher uniquely viable here: one spelling per construct means one canonical fix per error class; grammar isomorphic to the checked tree means node-path anchors are unambiguous; deterministic O(n) parsing with local error recovery eliminates heuristic error cascades — the classic failure mode where a confused checker teaches wrong lessons. The type-inference verdict's hard-error-on-ambiguity rule (disarming D009-class silent degradation) guarantees failures surface as diagnostics rather than silent behavior — everything the checker enforces is teachable because nothing fails silently. The marginal cost of repair-loop-primary is upgrading diagnostic PAYLOAD (attach rule fragment + counterexample + fix), not new subsystems.

4. SINGLE-SOURCING SPEC AND CHECKER KILLS DRIFT AND SURVIVES LANGUAGE CHURN. Factor the normative spec into addressable rule fragments compiled into the checker's error tables: the diagnostic quotes the fragment verbatim, so the taught spec and the enforced spec cannot diverge. This matters doubly because round 2 leaves open regimes (interior annotations, bounded quantification, region layer) — the language WILL churn. A resident mega-spec goes stale wholesale and teaches dead rules; a diagnostics-resident spec updates rule-by-rule with the checker, and the writer can only ever learn what the current checker actually enforces.

5. WHAT THE TOOLCHAIN MUST GUARANTEE (the position's obligations, stated as requirements): (a) Diagnostics-as-citations contract: every rejection class carries rule-fragment ID + verbatim rule text + elaborated view of the violating node + mechanical fix or edit list where derivable; no bare 'type error' exists. (b) Diagnostic completeness policed by a coverage suite: for every rule fragment, a negative test asserting the emitted diagnostic cites that fragment and its fix applies-and-checks — the direct generalization of validation-harness-plan.md's one-negative-test-per-feature rule. (c) Loop latency: content-addressed function-level memoized checking on canonical bytes (D001/D004 compile-time channel; modules verdict requirement) with reopen thresholds TIGHTENED relative to round 2, since this regime multiplies checker invocations; cache keys must remain exact semantic fingerprints (a weaker key is a miscompile vector per round2-modules.md, not an ergonomics bug). (d) Progress and stability: deterministic error ordering, batched reporting of independent violations (parallel teaching), and a no-oscillation property — executing an emitted edit list strictly shrinks the violation set or surfaces a diagnostic citing a different fragment. (e) Fix trustworthiness: mechanical fixes and edit lists are verified artifacts (fix-applies-and-typechecks in CI), because an authoritative teacher emitting a wrong fix is worse than no teacher. (f) Contract-edit gating (synthesis) integrated with fix suggestions, so the loop never teaches declaration-laundering as a repair strategy. (g) Runtime channel: trap-with-report with stack attribution (C002-adjacent, the only carded post-violation observability story) as the teaching channel for dynamic contract violations. (h) Lint-class feedback (region quality, sentinel encoding) promoted into the loop or explicitly assigned to the spec kernel — the checker can only teach what it detects.

6. THE PRINCIPLED SPEC/LOOP DIVISION. This is not zero-spec. The kernel teaches FORMS (grammar, annotation vocabulary shape, one complete worked example per construct: env-struct closure, operation table, Result propagation, generic instantiation, lifetime contract), because a model that cannot produce a first parse never reaches the teacher. The loop teaches RULES (checkable constraints). The line is measurable, not aesthetic: size the kernel to hit a pre-committed first-parse/first-elaboration success rate on the task battery; promote the head of the measured violation-frequency distribution into the kernel; leave the long tail to diagnostics. The kernel is also stable, hence prompt-cacheable across calls, while diagnostics are per-turn dynamic anyway — the hybrid exploits both cache regimes.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: SPARK precedent: a verification-first toolchain enforcing declared restrictions is the correctness locus, supporting the claim that the checker (not spec residency in writer context) is the safety mechanism, so lazy teaching cannot regress soundness.

Optimizer facts are UB-bearing promises sound only if checked; the hazard class is unchecked promises, which spec tokens cannot fix and the checker fixes regardless — grounding the safety asymmetry argument.

CONFIRMED (2026-07-02 adversarial verification). Compile time is an admissible AI cost channel; guaranteed monomorphization makes checking deterministic and cacheable, underwriting the fast-loop requirement.
Codegen units are build artifacts not semantics, so incrementality can be implementation-level — the basis for content-addressed memoized checking that keeps repair-loop latency acceptable.

(Direct-extraction, NOT in the 2026-07-02 verified batch — provisional.) Silent degradation is the archetype of an unteachable failure; the type-inference verdict's ambiguity-is-hard-error rule makes every enforced rule surface as a diagnostic, i.e., teachable.

(Provisional, direct-extraction.) Numeric modes are per-operation contracts; mode mismatch is a hard error — an example of a rule class ideally suited to loop teaching, versus wrong-but-well-typed mode choice which the loop cannot teach (concession 2).
Same as N001: one named mode per arithmetic node delineates the loop's teaching boundary between detectable mismatch and silent semantic wrongness.
The only carded post-violation observability precedent (race-detection halts / halt-with-report); anchors trap-with-report as the runtime end of the teaching channel.

CONFIRMED_WITH_AMENDED_SOURCES: compiler-derived capability facts are checker-trusted; supports routing fact production through the checker whose outputs (elaboration records) double as teaching artifacts.

Canonical single-spelling form, node-path diagnostics, deterministic local parse-error recovery, and content-addressed caching are decided obligations — the substrate that makes diagnostics unambiguous, cascade-free, and cheap enough to be the primary teacher; also names the training-distribution penalty decay experiment this position extends.

Requires content-addressed exact-semantic-fingerprint memoized checking with pre-committed latency reopen thresholds, cross-file provenance chains for nonlocal diagnostics, and contract-edit gating — the three toolchain guarantees this position tightens and makes load-bearing.

Trap-with-report + stack attribution as machine-readable diagnostics channel, and the conceded one-diagnostic-per-process-death loop cost that this position inherits and must price (multi-process harness wall-clock measurement becomes stricter).

Mandates the compiler-elaborated fully-annotated re-read artifact (the accepted-code teaching channel) and names the correlated-wrongness class (annotation and code both wrong, typechecks) that bounds what any checker-teacher can teach.

Six of eleven 'Additions required because the writer is an AI' (machine-readable diagnostics, edit lists, aggregation, elaboration artifact, redundancy channels, content-addressed caching) are already repair-loop infrastructure; also files the diagnostics repair-loop-asymmetry and closed-world compile-loop-pricing experiments this position extends.

The one-positive-one-negative-validation rule generalizes directly into the diagnostics-coverage suite: every spec rule fragment gets a negative test asserting its diagnostic cites the fragment and its fix applies.

D2 itself names 'progressive disclosure with compiler-as-teacher repair loops vs full-spec-in-context' as the delegated open question; this position is the compiler-as-teacher arm, argued under D2's two-budget framing.

concessions: First-attempt quality drops and iteration count rises by construction. Total-cost dominance is NOT established: each iteration costs diagnostic re-read + code re-emission + check latency, and if violation density is high (the surface is deliberately alien — canonical form, env-structs, explicit modes are all off training distribution), the loop can cost more total tokens and wall-clock than a resident spec. The regime is a bet on steep within-session learning, not a proven economy.

The checker cannot teach intent. Wrong-but-well-typed states — wrong numeric mode that typechecks, poor region assignment, and especially the type-inference verdict's correlated-wrongness class (annotation and code both wrong, mutually consistent) — never produce a diagnostic, so lazy teaching never touches them. The spec kernel must still carry semantic-intent guidance and idiom examples for these classes, which puts a hard floor under spec minimality. The position's honest scope is: the loop teaches checkable constraints; it teaches nothing else.

Amortization can fail across sessions. A resident spec is paid once per context; a diagnostic-taught rule is re-taught in every fresh session that violates it. If fresh agents recur on the same head-of-distribution violations, lazy teaching converges to paying the spec cost anyway — in installments, plus checker latency. The mitigation (harvesting frequently-cited fragments into the prompt, i.e., the loop generating a personalized spec) is a workflow pattern, not a language guarantee, and pushes toward the hybrid rather than the pure position.

Prompt caching weakens the raw dollar-cost argument for spec minimalism: a stable full spec is one-time cacheable. The surviving D2 costs are context-window ceiling and long-context retrieval quality, which are real but narrower than 'spec tokens are expensive' suggests.

First-parse entry is spec's job, irreducibly. A model that cannot emit parseable canonical form never reaches the teacher, so the kernel must guarantee a high first-parse rate with examples spanning every construct — conceding the spec-primary side's strongest point at the boundary: forms are taught up front, only rules are taught lazily.

Diagnostic engineering at citation grade for every rejection class is a large, unpriced toolchain investment (rule factoring, counterexample synthesis, fix derivation, coverage tests), and it lands on the same team building the checker, elaborator, provenance, and multi-process harness that round 2 already mandated.
risks: Teacher-error risk: a wrong mechanical fix or stale rule citation is an authoritative teacher instilling a false rule; edit lists become trusted artifacts requiring their own verification (fix-applies-and-typechecks CI), otherwise the regime actively poisons the writer's model of the language.

Local-fix myopia: repair loops optimize for making the checker accept, not for quality — e.g., cloning or over-copying to silence an ownership error instead of restructuring, sacrificing the extreme-performance goal invisibly. Legality is taught; performance is not. Requires lint/performance-report channels in the loop, whose in-loop effectiveness for AI writers is itself untested (synthesis experiment line on lint-class feedback).

Nonlocal-error teachability: closed-world ownership/aliasing violations surface far from cause; rendering them as a teachable fragment + fix depends on cross-file provenance chains whose machine-repairability is an open modules-verdict experiment. If nonlocal diagnostics are not repairable, the hardest rules are exactly the ones the loop teaches worst.

Oscillation/thrash: fixing rule A's violation can surface rule B's, and a naive loop can cycle; without deterministic error ordering, batching, and a monotonic-progress guarantee the loop's economics collapse.

Latency dependence is single-point-of-failure: the whole regime rides on the closed-world whole-program check being fast at AI-scale program sizes (unpriced; modules verdict's biggest admitted unknown). If content-addressed memoization underdelivers, iterations multiply against a slow checker and the regime loses to spec-primary on wall-clock alone. Trap=abort's one-diagnostic-per-process-death compounds this for runtime-taught rules.

Spec/checker drift if rule fragments are not single-sourced: a diagnostic teaching a rule the checker does not enforce (or vice versa) is a systemic trust failure; the single-sourcing requirement is load-bearing, not optional.

Distribution-shift blind spot: the kernel-sizing rule depends on a measured violation-frequency distribution from a task battery; real optimizer workloads may have a different head, leaving the kernel mis-sized and the loop overloaded on rules assumed rare.
empiricalUntested: FLAGSHIP — Lazy-vs-resident teaching A/B: arms (a) full spec in context, (b) kernel + spec-citing diagnostics, (c) kernel + bare diagnostics (ablating the citation payload). Measure iterations-to-green, total tokens (prefix + transcript + re-emissions), wall-clock including checks, and residual silent-defect rate post-green. Every claim that just-in-time diagnostic fragments teach better than resident spec text is untested; this experiment decides the debate. Extends the synthesis 'diagnostics repair-loop asymmetry' item.

Within-session transfer: does a diagnostic about rule R at site S reduce R-violations at OTHER sites later in the same session (i.e., does error-message learning generalize beyond the repaired site)? The lazy-teaching economics assume yes; no card supports it. Measure violation rate of R before vs after first R-citation.

Violation-density census: generate a program battery from kernel-only spec and histogram rule violations by frequency. Tests the assumed steep (head/tail) distribution and empirically sizes the kernel: head promoted to resident spec, tail left to the loop.

Cross-session recurrence: do fresh sessions/agents repeat the same violation head (amortization failure)? Decides whether taught fragments must be harvested into the prompt, i.e., whether the pure position degrades to the hybrid.

Diagnostic payload dose-response: rule text alone vs +counterexample vs +mechanical fix vs +edit list; measure fix success rate, repair latency, and regression rate (repairs breaking other sites). Engineering-cost allocation across the payload tiers depends on this.

First-parse entry threshold: minimum kernel (grammar + N examples) achieving target first-parse/first-elaboration success on canonical form — extends the syntax verdict's training-distribution-penalty-decay experiment; sets the irreducible spec floor.

Repair-loop-multiplied compile pricing: the modules/synthesis closed-world compile-loop pricing experiment re-run with realistic repair-iteration multipliers and content-addressed caching enabled; pre-commit tightened reopen thresholds, since this position increases checker invocations per unit of shipped code.

Lint-actionability in-loop (already filed in synthesis): whether AI writers act effectively on non-error lint feedback (region quality, sentinel encoding, timeliness) during repair — determines whether the loop can teach quality or only legality.

Multi-process trap-harness wall-clock per detected defect (error-handling verdict follow-up) re-scoped under repair-loop-primary load: the regime leans harder on runtime teaching for contract violations, so the acceptability threshold tightens.

Fix-trust calibration: rate at which models over-trust wrong or suboptimal mechanical fixes versus reasoning from the cited rule text — decides whether fixes should be emitted as commands or as suggestions with rule text primary.

result.position: The teaching burden should live in neither a hand-written in-context spec nor the interactive repair loop as primary channel. The toolchain must maintain a GENERATED, always-current teaching pack — a spec kernel derived from the grammar/checker rule set, N canonical task-shaped examples auto-extracted from the verified codebase, and the complete diagnostics vocabulary with repair exemplars — content-addressed, sized to a hard token budget on real tokenizers, and regenerated on every language change. In-context teaching becomes a build artifact with its own tests: models must pass pack-taught generation benchmarks before a language change ships. The spec/repair-loop split is then a measured, versioned quantity set by the benchmark, not a design-time constant.
result.coreArgument:
1) THE PACK IS A COROLLARY OF DECIDED FLOORS, NOT A NEW ARCHITECTURE. Every property the pack needs was already ruled into law for other reasons. Canonical form with exactly one spelling per construct and a fully elaborated stored artifact (round2-syntax 'What must be true'; synthesis SURFACE line) means every verified program in the codebase is a spec-consistent example BY CONSTRUCTION — example mining is deterministic content-addressed subtree selection, with no normalization choices and no risk that examples teach a dialect the spec forbids. Content-addressed caching on canonical bytes (synthesis, 'Additions required') gives dedup and coverage bookkeeping for free. The modules verdict already invented the exact mechanism I generalize: compiler-GENERATED token-compact interface views, produced on demand, never stored, zero semantic weight — the teaching pack is that mechanism applied to the language itself instead of a module. And the machine-readable diagnostics contract already forces a finite, closed diagnostics vocabulary to exist; publishing it in the pack is marginal cost. The synthesis's own warning about consistency-layer proliferation (open tension: six drift mechanisms must unify over one canonical substrate) is honored: the pack generator is an instance of mechanism 2, provenance-checked deterministic generation, riding the same substrate — not a seventh subsystem.

2) AGAINST THE PURE-SPEC POLE. Under D2, spec tokens are re-paid in EVERY fresh context, so spec size is a budget with regression pressure, like binary size. A hand-maintained prose spec cannot be size-gated or currency-checked: it is a second spelling of the language with no checker, i.e., exactly the drift class the toolchain-boundary policing rule forbids in source ('no second stored spelling'). The language is explicitly unfinished — the synthesis names pending holes (interior-annotation regime, bounded quantification, FFI wall) that WILL change the surface — so a static spec is stale precisely during the period the language is taught most. The modules verdict already rejected hand-maintained interface files in favor of generated, re-verified views for this same reason (D004/D007/D008 boundary-staleness logic); the argument lifts one level. A generated spec kernel derived from the grammar and checker rules is the only spec whose currency is machine-checkable. Note also that D2 retroactively pays a dividend on round-2's cuts: no macros, no closures-as-feature, no sugar, no implicit conversions, mono-only generics — every cut feature is spec tokens the pack never carries. No floor needs reopening; the floors are what make a small pack possible.

3) AGAINST THE PURE-REPAIR POLE. (a) Repair teaching is paid per code unit per iteration, and lands on a loop whose economics are already the knowingly-accepted risk of trap=abort: one diagnostic per process death, wall-clock must be measured acceptable or the checked-tier question reopens (round2-error-handling 'What must be true'; synthesis 'Additions required'). Pushing first-contact language teaching into that loop multiplies iterations exactly where the design has least slack. (b) Structurally, diagnostics can only teach negations — what the checker rejects. The corpus already catalogs well-typed-but-wrong states that surface only as lints or never (region-assignment quality, reclamation timeliness, sentinel encoding), and performance idioms — env-struct encodings, operation-table shapes, lifetime-contract spellings — never surface as errors at all. Only a curriculum teaches positive idioms. (c) Even the repair pole needs the pack: a diagnostic is a teaching signal only if its vocabulary is in-distribution, which means the vocabulary must be taught in-context anyway. (d) Site-enumerated edit lists are load-bearing for O(K) closed-set propagation, but an edit list is executable only by a writer who already knows how to write an arm body — edit lists relocate writing, they do not teach it. So the repair loop is the pack's RUNTIME, and the pack must teach the loop: one repair exemplar per diagnostic class, making the loop itself in-distribution.

4) THE TESTED-ARTIFACT CLAUSE IS THE ONLY EMPIRICALLY HONEST ANSWER TO THE TOPIC. How much burden moves to the repair loop is a property of models, and D2's own text plus the corpus standing rule (caveats.md: cite cards or it does not ship; all AI-behavior claims EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED) forbid deciding it by fiat. Both pole positions hard-code an untested behavioral claim into the architecture. My position institutionalizes the measurement instead: a held-out generation benchmark gates every language change (extending validation-harness-plan.md's rule — no promotion without positive and negative validation — to the teaching layer), and the pack generator has a tunable budget knob, so the toolchain can sweep pack size against total tokens-to-green (pack + program + repair traffic) and sit at the knee of the curve. This also permanently institutionalizes measurements round 2 already ordered as one-offs: round2-syntax follow-up 4 ('how fast the training-distribution gap closes with in-context examples'), follow-up 5 (the syntax/AI-codegen evidence lane), and the synthesis's diagnostics repair-loop asymmetry experiment. Language evolution gets a priced teachability cost channel — exactly what D2 demands — and a teachability regression becomes a build break instead of a silent fleet-wide degradation (the D009 silent-degradation pattern, transposed from types to curriculum).

5) WHAT THE TOOLCHAIN MUST GUARANTEE (the topic's second half): (i) deterministic, provenance-recorded pack generation — pinned generator version, content-addressed pack, regeneration diff on every build (mechanism 2 of the unified consistency layer); (ii) every example extracted from the verified codebase and RE-VERIFIED on every regeneration — no hand-authored prose code blocks in the pack, ever; (iii) a hard pack budget measured on real model tokenizers, not regex proxies (the syntax verdict's own 1.35–1.48x proxy caveat), with budget regressions failing the build; (iv) computable coverage — every grammar production, every diagnostic code, every mandated idiom (env-struct call interface, operation tables, Result propagation with auto-derived context, lifetime contracts, explicit conversions) covered by at least one task-shaped example, which is only computable because closed-world + canonical form make these closed sets; (v) diagnostics vocabulary as versioned API — renaming a diagnostic is a pack-invalidating, benchmark-gated change; (vi) a held-out, rotated benchmark task set with pre-committed thresholds, never included in the pack (contamination discipline); (vii) governance: the pack is the sole in-context teaching artifact of record, mirroring contract-edit gating — a language change cannot ship by editing the pack to route around a failing benchmark.

RESULTING SPLIT: the pack front-loads grammar kernel, contract/annotation vocabulary, idiom exemplars, and diagnostics vocabulary; the repair loop carries codebase-specific facts (via generated interface views, not the pack), nonlocal provenance-chained errors, and closed-set edit propagation. That boundary is re-measured every regeneration — the division of labor is an output of the toolchain, not an input to the language.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Decides canonical form (one spelling, byte-level formatting, tree-isomorphic grammar) and fully elaborated stored artifacts — the property that makes auto-extracted examples spec-consistent by construction; its AI-writer section documents the regex-proxy tokenizer caveat my budget guarantee corrects for; follow-ups 4 and 5 explicitly order measurement of how fast in-context examples close the training-distribution gap, which the pack benchmark institutionalizes.

Supplies the load-bearing infrastructure: unified consistency layer mechanism 2 (provenance-checked deterministic generation) which the pack generator instantiates; compiler-generated token-compact interface views (the pack's direct precedent); machine-readable diagnostics as universal contract (the vocabulary the pack publishes); site-enumerated edit lists (shown to relocate rather than teach writing); contract-edit gating (precedent for gating privileged artifact edits); the consistency-layer proliferation tension my design must not worsen; and the multi-process loop-economics cost that makes repair-loop teaching expensive.

'What must be true' concedes the repair loop's economics are already at risk under trap=abort (one diagnostic per process death, wall-clock must be acceptable or checked-tier reopens) — the strongest structural reason not to move first-contact teaching burden into that loop.

D2 defines the two budgets the pack manages and amends 'verbosity is free'; D0 makes toolchain/machine costs (pack generation, benchmark runs) admissible cost channels; D2's delegation text names progressive disclosure vs full-spec-in-context as the open question this position answers with a third, generated artifact.
Establishes the project norm that nothing promotes without positive and negative validation sketches; the pack-benchmark CI gate is this rule extended to the teaching layer.

Standing rule that proposals must cite cards and mark unproven claims — the argument that the spec/repair split must be a measured quantity, not fiat, is this rule applied to teaching-burden allocation.

Codegen-unit/incremental/LTO evidence that compile-time is a carded, admissible cost/tradeoff channel — pricing pack regeneration and content-addressed caching in the iteration loop as legitimate toolchain costs.

Monomorphization's compile-time/code-size tradeoff is the carded precedent for treating a one-time generation cost (here: pack tokens paid per context) as a budgeted, measured tradeoff rather than free or forbidden.

Julia's type-instability guidance is the corpus's silent-degradation pattern: implicit, unchecked dependencies degrade without local errors — the same failure mode as a stale hand-maintained spec or an untested repair-taught curriculum, which the regeneration+benchmark gate converts into a loud build break.
The only carded post-violation observability precedent (halt-with-report) underwrites the machine-readable diagnostics channel whose closed vocabulary the pack publishes and exemplifies.

SPARK is carded precedent that a verification-first language restricts surface expressiveness to keep the source-to-logic mapping simple — the same restriction that makes a small, generatable spec kernel and a closed coverage metric possible.

SIL's lesson — keep performance-critical semantics visible to tools rather than buried — is the design pattern the pack generalizes: teaching-critical language knowledge is made a visible, tool-maintained artifact instead of an ambient assumption about model memory.

InstCombine's discipline (canonicalization transforms carry their own validation obligations) is precedent that generated/derived artifacts in the trust path need their own tests — the pack's generation benchmarks are that obligation applied to the curriculum.

Halide's algorithm/schedule separation is carded precedent for moving a concern (here: curriculum construction and sizing) out of language semantics into a tooling layer with its own search/measurement loop, exactly where the synthesis already routes schedule search.

concessions: Bootstrap circularity: 'auto-extracted from the verified codebase' is impossible before a verified codebase exists. Pack v0 must be seeded with hand-written (machine-verified) examples, so the position's purity holds only at steady state; early packs carry exactly the hand-maintenance drift risk I argue against, mitigated only by re-verification.

A verified subtree is not automatically pedagogical: extraction guarantees correctness and spec-consistency, not task-shapedness or representativeness. The selection policy (which N, at what granularity, decontextualized how) is a hard open problem and the weakest link in the proposal.

The pack does not dissolve D2's budget pressure, it manages it: the pack sits in every context, so if the empirical knee of the pack-size/repair-iterations curve is large, per-task token pressure returns undiminished and the pure-repair pole regains force for that regime.

Gating language changes on model benchmarks couples language evolution to specific model versions and vendors; a pass on model X says nothing carded about model Y, and this is a real governance cost the pure-spec pole does not pay.

This is a substantial new toolchain subsystem (generator, coverage computation, benchmark harness, CI gate) added to a design the synthesis already warns is proliferating consistency machinery; if the pack generator does not ride the canonical-artifact substrate and provenance mechanism, it worsens the exact tension it claims to honor.

The repair loop remains load-bearing under my position — nonlocal provenance-chained errors, edit lists, and lint-class feedback still do teaching-adjacent work per code unit; I claim the pack minimizes that traffic, but if the diagnostics-exemplar effect is small, the marginal value of the pack over spec-kernel-plus-good-diagnostics shrinks toward tooling overhead.

Benchmark-thresholds-as-policy is unavoidable: too strict blocks changes the design needs (e.g., closing the FFI-wall hole), too loose gates nothing; the gate's value depends entirely on threshold discipline the corpus cannot yet inform.
risks: Goodhart/overfitting: tuning the pack generator against the gating benchmark selects packs that maximize benchmark pass rather than real-task competence; requires held-out task rotation whose own effectiveness is untested.

Ossification to current models: a benchmark-gated language may reject changes that are teachable to future models but not today's, freezing design around present-day model quirks — the training-distribution penalty concern from round2-syntax, promoted from cost to veto power.
Gate erosion: model-benchmark CI runs are slow, costly, and flaky; if teams batch changes or bypass the gate under schedule pressure, a bypassed gate yields false confidence — worse than no gate.

Regeneration churn: 'always-current' means the pack changes even when semantics do not (re-extraction picks different exemplars); if model output is sensitive to example ordering/identity, regeneration itself perturbs fleet behavior unpredictably.

Second-spec authority conflict: the pack becomes a de facto normative spec; divergence between it and any prose normative document creates a source-of-record ambiguity mirroring the elaborator-codec tension the synthesis already flags as unresolved.

Benchmark contamination via the codebase: extracted examples come from the same corpus that generated benchmark tasks; leakage silently inflates pass rates unless extraction and task pools are partitioned, adding process burden.

Load concentration: the pack depends on canonical form, closed-world coverage sets, and the diagnostics contract all landing as ruled; the synthesis notes these floors rest on a handful of unverified cards (D001 flagged priority) — if adversarial verification displaces one, the pack's guarantees weaken with it.
empiricalUntested: EU-1: That in-context spec+examples suffice for current models to write this canonical-form language at usable quality at all (D2's operational premise). Experiment: pack-taught generation benchmark v0 on kernel tasks; measure first-parse success, silent-semantic-error rate via differential testing, iterations-to-green — extends round2-syntax follow-up 1 and the training-distribution-penalty measurement.

EU-2: That task-shaped examples outperform prose rules per token for in-context teaching (justifies the example-heavy pack). Experiment: fixed total budget, sweep spec:example ratio (100:0 to 0:100) on the same held-out benchmark.

EU-3: That total tokens-to-green (pack + program + repair traffic) has a knee as pack budget grows (justifies the tunable-budget architecture and answers the topic's 'how much moves to repair' quantitatively). Experiment: pack-size sweep with repair-traffic accounting.

EU-4: That incorrect or atypical in-context examples measurably degrade generation (justifies mandatory re-verification of extracted examples). Experiment: adversarial pack injection — subtly wrong but plausible examples; measure error-uptake rate.

EU-5: That publishing the diagnostics vocabulary with one repair exemplar per class improves repair success and latency versus diagnostics alone (justifies the pack teaching the loop). Experiment: repair-loop A/B with/without the vocabulary section — extends the synthesis's diagnostics repair-loop asymmetry experiment.

EU-6: That pack benchmark results are stable across model families and versions (gates the CI-gate policy itself). Experiment: run the unchanged-language benchmark across >=3 model families and successive versions; measure pass-flip rate; pre-commit a stability threshold below which the gate demotes to advisory.
EU-7: That auto-extracted examples match hand-curated ones at equal budget (justifies auto-extraction at steady state). Experiment: pack A/B, mined vs curated, same budget, same benchmark.

EU-8: That models act on the pack's positive-idiom exemplars for well-typed-but-wrong states (regions, sentinels) that diagnostics cannot teach. Experiment: idiom-adherence audit of generated code with vs without idiom exemplars in the pack — couples to the synthesis's lint-actionability question.

EU-9: That regeneration churn (semantically neutral example re-selection/reordering) does not perturb generation quality (gates the 'always-current' property). Experiment: A/B semantically identical packs with permuted/re-mined examples; measure output divergence and defect-rate delta.

result.position: Spec-primary: the one-time in-context spec plus a curated example set carries essentially all of the teaching burden. The interactive repair loop is scoped to (a) residual defects, (b) program-specific closed-set propagation (edit lists after variant/contract changes), and (c) offline telemetry that improves the spec — it is never the channel by which the model learns the language's semantics. Measured first-pass correctness (first-parse rate, first-verify rate, first-semantic-correct rate) is the primary design metric for the spec, the example set, and the surface language itself.
result.coreArgument:
Four arguments, in descending order of strength.

(1) THE COST STRUCTURE OF D2 FAVORS FRONT-LOADING. D2 defines two budgets: SPEC (one-time per context) and PROGRAM (per code unit). Repair-loop teaching converts a one-time cost into a recurring one: every round-trip re-enters context as wrong code + diagnostic + fix, and it recurs per unit, per error class, per session. Spec tokens amortize over every unit generated under that context; repair tokens compound with iteration count. The corpus already treats loop round-trips as a priced, at-risk channel, not a free resource: the modules verdict names closed-world compile latency 'the single largest real cost and it is currently unpriced'; the error-handling verdict only 'knowingly accepted' one-diagnostic-per-process-death for traps, with wall-clock measurement as an explicit reopen trigger; the synthesis mandates content-addressed caching precisely to keep the loop cheap (D001/D004 compile-time channel). A design that additionally routes TEACHING through that loop multiplies exposure to the exact cost the round-2 verdicts flagged as their biggest unpriced risk. Worse, repair-loop 'learning' is context-fragile: keep the repair transcript and it permanently taxes the PROGRAM budget of every subsequent unit; evict it and the lesson is gone. If a repair lesson is worth retaining, the correct sink is distillation back into the spec/examples — which is spec-primary by construction.

(2) ERROR-DRIVEN LEARNING IS BIASED TOWARD WHAT THE CHECKER CATCHES LOUDEST, AND THE CORPUS PROVES THE CHECKER IS SYSTEMATICALLY SILENT SOMEWHERE. A model taught by diagnostics learns the checker's error surface, not the language's semantics. The round-2 record enumerates the silent classes: correlated wrongness where annotation and code are both wrong and typecheck (round2-type-inference); coherently-hallucinated caller/callee pairs that regenerate together and ship (round2-modules); well-typed-but-wrong states explicitly relegated to lint-class feedback — region-assignment quality, reclamation timeliness, in-band sentinel encoding, one-big-arena pessimization (synthesis, memory-automation); D009-class silent degradation where the failure produces no local diagnostic at all. In a repair-primary regime these classes receive ZERO teaching signal while D1's deliberately loud ownership/race checker dominates the gradient — the model over-fits to borrow-check-shaped fixes and under-learns semantic intent, numeric-mode choice (N001/N002), and cost contracts. Only spec and examples can teach what the checker cannot see, and the checker-invisible residue is precisely where this project's remaining risk lives. [EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED as a model-behavior claim; checker-shadow experiment below.]

(3) THE ROUND-2 FLOORS ALREADY BOUGHT US A SPEC-COMPRESSIBLE, FIRST-PASS-FRIENDLY LANGUAGE; SPEC-PRIMARY CASHES THAT IN. Every decided floor shrinks either the spec or the error space: canonical single-spelling form means one grammar with no alternatives to specify and eliminates the precedence-misbinding class by construction rather than by repair (round2-syntax); zero implicit conversions and no implicit dispatch make meaning local and compositional — there are no hidden coercion-resolution orders or inference fallbacks to discover interactively (round2-type-inference explicitly credits the cut with 'no need for the writer to simulate coercion-resolution order'); mono-only generics with explicit instantiation remove bound-solving behavior that could only be learned by probing; deterministic ambiguity-rejecting synthesis means the compiler has no heuristic personality to explore. F006/SPARK is the carded precedent: verification-first languages restrict surface expressiveness to keep the source-to-logic mapping simple — the same restriction keeps the source-to-SPEC mapping small. A language whose semantics are heuristic-free is a language whose complete behavior is statable up front; repair-loop discovery is only load-bearing for languages with emergent behavior, which round-2 systematically cut. There is also a structural rhyme with the corpus's founding thesis: J001/J002 show learning-by-observation (runtime specialization + guards) is conditional recovery machinery with warmup and deopt cliffs, and this project chose static contracts over profile-driven recovery everywhere. Spec-primary is the same choice applied to teaching: front-loaded declared facts over interactive rediscovery. (Analogy, not evidence.)

(4) FIRST-PASS CORRECTNESS IS THE ONLY METRIC THAT PRICES BOTH BUDGETS AT ONCE, AND THE EXPERIMENT PROGRAM ALREADY REACHES FOR IT. The round-2 experiments independently converged on 'first-parse success rate', 'iterations-to-green', 'repair iterations', and 'tokens generated+re-read' as endpoints (syntax A/B, flagship drift experiment, redundancy-independence). Spec-primary elevates first-pass correctness to the design objective: a spec/example-set/surface-form decision is good iff it measurably raises first-pass correctness per spec token spent. The syntax verdict's follow-up — measure the training-distribution penalty and 'how fast the gap closes with in-context examples' — is already the spec-dose experiment this position needs.

WHAT THE TOOLCHAIN MUST GUARANTEE UNDER SPEC-PRIMARY (the topic's second question). The synthesis's diagnostics apparatus is kept wholesale but re-scoped from teaching channel to repair channel, and five guarantees are added: (i) EXECUTABLE SPEC CONFORMANCE — the spec is a build artifact, version-locked to the compiler; every normative statement has a conformance test and every spec example compiles in CI, because in a spec-primary regime a spec/implementation divergence destroys first-pass correctness with no in-loop recovery (this is the syntax verdict's second-spelling policing applied to the spec itself). (ii) SPEC-ANCHORED DIAGNOSTICS — machine-readable node-path diagnostics and site-enumerated edit lists (already law per synthesis/round2-dynamic-dispatch) additionally cite stable spec anchors, so residual repair re-grounds in already-paid spec tokens instead of importing new teaching text per error. (iii) ZERO-INFERENCE REPAIR as the diagnostic design bar — the fix must be derivable from diagnostic + spec anchor without language re-learning; edit lists after closed-set changes are the exemplar (file/site/missing-arm tuples). (iv) DETERMINISTIC, HEURISTIC-FREE CHECKER BEHAVIOR — no observable behavior may exist only in the implementation; anything discoverable-by-probing is a spec bug. (v) FIRST-PASS TELEMETRY AND SPEC-SLICE GENERATION — the toolchain records first-pass rates per construct and mines repair transcripts OFFLINE to improve spec/examples (the loop teaches the spec maintainers, not the in-context model); and, like the modules verdict's compiler-generated interface views, the compiler can emit deterministic task-relevant spec slices — progressive disclosure is admissible as spec PACKAGING, never as repair-loop discovery. The mandated elaborated re-read artifact and content-addressed caching (synthesis) remain load-bearing: they keep per-unit program context cheap so the spec fits, which is the D2 budget interaction that makes spec-primary affordable.

ANSWER TO THE TOPIC QUESTION: the teaching burden that moves to the repair loop is limited to program-specific, spec-inexpressible facts — the enumeration of THIS program's obligations after a closed-set change — plus correction of residual defects. Language semantics never move there. If the measured minimal complete spec of the round-2 core exceeds a practical spec budget, that is the pre-committed reopen condition for this ruling, and the fallback is spec-slicing by task profile, not compiler-as-teacher.

Trap=abort gives one diagnostic per process death with wall-clock measurement as an explicit reopen trigger; C002 is the only carded post-violation observability precedent. The carded compile-time cost channel (monomorphization expansion; codegen-unit/LTO tradeoff) that makes every extra repair iteration a real wall-clock and cache-churn cost, and that motivates the synthesis's content-addressed caching requirement my position depends on.

Its toolchain-boundary policing (no second stored spelling; bijective, fixpoint-checked codecs) is the template for my executable-spec-conformance guarantee.

The 'Additions required because the writer is an AI' section (machine-readable node-path diagnostics, site-enumerated edit lists, mandatory elaborated re-read artifact, content-addressed caching, lint-class feedback for well-typed-but-wrong states) is the repair apparatus I keep wholesale but re-scope as correction, not teaching; its well-typed-but-wrong inventory (region quality, reclamation timeliness, sentinels) is my checker-shadow evidence.

Site-enumerated edit lists as 'a mechanical repair plan... executable by a fallible writer' define the legitimate repair-loop role under spec-primary: enumeration of program-specific closed-set obligations, presupposing — not producing — language knowledge.

SPARK precedent: verification-first languages restrict surface expressiveness to keep the source-to-logic mapping simple — the same restriction keeps the source-to-spec mapping small, grounding the claim that the round-2 core is spec-compressible.

Named, truthfully-lowered numeric modes with zero implicit conversions make semantics local and statable — no hidden per-operation contract reassignment exists to be discovered by trial, so the numeric surface is teachable entirely up front.

Carded pattern that learning-by-observation (specialization + guards/deopt) is conditional recovery machinery with warmup and cliff costs — the corpus's founding reason to prefer static contracts over profile-driven recovery, structurally rhyming with spec-primary over repair-discovery (cited as pattern, not proof).

Requires exact-card citation and explicit unproven-claim flagging; since the corpus has zero AI-codegen or in-context-learning cards, it forces every model-behavior claim in this debate — mine and my opponent's — into the EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED bucket with named experiments.

Establishes the house rule that design candidates need positive and negative empirical validation before promotion — the procedural hook for making first-pass-correctness measurement (spec-dose, repair-transfer, checker-shadow) the gate for this ruling.

concessions: The corpus contains ZERO cards on AI code generation or in-context learning of novel languages (caveats.md; every round-2 verdict repeats it). My position's central behavioral claims — spec front-loading yields higher first-pass correctness than repair-loop teaching, and error-driven learning is checker-biased — are exactly as untested as my opponent's. This debate can only be settled by the named experiments, so my verdict must ship as a leading candidate with pre-committed reopen thresholds, not as law.

Point-of-error delivery is strictly superior for program-specific facts: no spec can enumerate THIS program's K switch sites after a variant addition. I concede the entire closed-set-propagation domain to edit lists (round2-dynamic-dispatch), and concede that this domain may account for a large fraction of total loop traffic in practice.

Spec compression has a floor I cannot currently state: a complete spec of the round-2 core — ownership/borrowing at Rust-class rigor (D1), effect rows, numeric modes, contract groups, canonical grammar — may be large, and in-context comprehension of long formal specs is itself EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED. If the measured minimal spec exceeds practical context budgets, some deferral to disclosure/repair is forced and my position must yield ground on schedule, not principle.

'One-time' spec cost is really per-context: multi-agent and multi-session workflows re-pay it on every fresh context. Prompt caching mitigates this but is infrastructure-dependent, priced by vendors, and uncarded — my amortization arithmetic is sensitive to it.

The toolchain does not get simpler under spec-primary. Every diagnostics obligation in the synthesis remains mandatory (residual errors still need machine-executable repair), and I ADD spec-conformance CI, spec anchoring, and telemetry. My position re-scopes the apparatus; it cannot claim to shrink it.

Repair transcripts have real teaching value — offline. Mining them to improve the spec/example set concedes that the loop is the best available measurement instrument for where the spec fails; the disagreement with my opponent is about the sink (spec maintainers vs the in-context model), not about whether the loop generates knowledge.

First-pass correctness may plateau below acceptable even with a perfect spec — current models may simply need interactive calibration on an alien canonical form (the syntax verdict's admissible, unquantified training-distribution penalty). If the spec-dose experiment shows a hard plateau that repair iterations reliably break, the burden split must move toward the loop.
risks: Spec/implementation drift becomes a first-class hazard my position creates: a wrong or stale spec teaches wrong semantics confidently, and — by my own checker-shadow argument — the checker will not catch the semantic residue. Mitigation is executable spec conformance (every normative statement tested, every example compiled, spec version-locked to compiler), which is real new toolchain surface that can itself rot; this is the syntax verdict's second-spelling problem reincarnated at the spec level.

Goodharting the metric: making measured first-pass correctness THE design objective risks bending language decisions toward what current models find easy over what the optimizer and D1 require. First-pass correctness must remain strictly subordinate to D0/D1 and the decided floors — it selects among admissible designs, never against them — and the metric must be re-run as models change, or the language fossilizes around one model generation's quirks.

Over-compression risk: an aggressively compressed spec can induce systematic misgeneralization (plausible-but-wrong priors imported from training-adjacent languages filling the gaps), producing exactly the coherent, checker-passing wrongness the corpus fears most (round2-modules hallucinated-pair class). Compression must be validated by semantic differential testing, not token count alone.

Context-economics nonstationarity: the whole amortization case rests on 2026 pricing of context, caching, and round-trip latency. Cheap persistent caches or vastly longer contexts would weaken the recurring-cost argument against repair-primary; conversely, tighter budgets strengthen it. The ruling should state its economic assumptions so it can be mechanically re-opened.

Session-shape sensitivity: if real workloads are many short single-unit sessions, the per-session spec tax dominates and spec-primary loses its own arithmetic; if they are long multi-unit agentic runs, it wins. The prevalence question (analogous to the dispatch verdict's open-vs-closed site census) is unmeasured.

The multi-process trap=abort harness wall-clock question (round2-error-handling reopen trigger) compounds with this ruling: if that measurement fails AND teaching load was kept off the loop, the loop still becomes the bottleneck for residual repair alone — my position reduces but does not eliminate exposure to that open fork.
empiricalUntested: SPEC-DOSE/RESPONSE (the deciding experiment): identical task batteries under S0 (examples only), S1 (compressed spec), S2 (full spec + curated examples), S1/S2 plus repair loop; measure first-parse rate, first-verify rate, semantic correctness via differential testing, iterations-to-green, and TOTAL tokens (spec + program + repair transcript). Tests the core claim that front-loaded spec minimizes total cost per correct unit; extends the round2-syntax follow-up on training-distribution penalty decay with in-context examples.

REPAIR-TRANSFER: after an error class is fixed via diagnostic in unit N, measure recurrence of that class in units N+1..N+k (a) with the repair transcript retained in context, (b) with it evicted, (c) with it distilled into one added spec/example line. Directly tests the opposing position's premise that the loop teaches durably, and my claim that distillation dominates retention.

CHECKER-SHADOW: compare spec-light+repair-heavy vs spec-heavy regimes on the rate of checker-INVISIBLE defects — correlated wrongness (round2-type-inference), coherently-hallucinated caller/callee pairs (round2-modules), region-quality/reclamation/sentinel states (synthesis lint classes) — via differential testing and lint audit. Tests my claim that error-driven learning under-corrects exactly where the checker is silent.

SPEC-SIZE FLOOR: actually write the minimal complete spec of the round-2 core (canonical grammar, ownership/borrow rules, modes, effects, contract groups, diagnostics vocabulary) and token-count it under real model tokenizers per target model; pre-commit the budget threshold above which this ruling's fallback (task-profile spec slices) or partial deferral to the loop is triggered.

DIAGNOSTIC-ANCHOR ABLATION: bare node-path diagnostics vs diagnostics carrying stable spec anchors vs diagnostics carrying inlined teaching text; measure repair success rate, iterations, and per-error token cost. Decides whether residual repair can re-ground in already-paid spec tokens (my guarantee ii) or needs fresh teaching tokens per error (opponent's channel).

FIRST-PASS ENDPOINT RETROFIT: add first-pass compile/verify/semantic-correct rates as primary endpoints to the already-planned flagship drift experiment, redundancy-independence experiment, and syntax A/B (they currently measure iterations and tokens but not first-pass rates as the headline metric).

SPEC-CONFORMANCE EFFICACY: inject deliberate spec/implementation divergences and measure (a) how fast conformance CI catches them and (b) the first-pass-correctness damage per undetected divergence-day — prices my position's biggest self-created risk.

Standing flags inherited from the corpus: the multi-process harness wall-clock measurement (round2-error-handling), closed-world compile-loop pricing (round2-modules), and elaborated-view re-read error rates (round2-type-inference) all remain EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED and all condition how expensive each residual repair round-trip is — every one of them moves the spec-vs-loop breakeven point.

challenges.challenge: The opening 'physics' is two uncarded model-behavior claims stated as fact: that the spec is 're-paid in every context window' (workflow-dependent — prompt caching and persistent sessions can amortize it; the position's own E-list and Position 3's E6 flag this untested) and that in-window spec bytes 'compete byte-for-byte' with task context (attention-dilution, uncarded; round2-metaprogramming.md line 35 explicitly records 'no cards exist' for context-length effects). If caching amortizes and the dose-response curve is flat, the cap's entire cost model reduces to its weakest-evidenced leg. The position flags the dose-response experiment but does not present its core argument as conditional on it.

Claiming the budget 'permanently closes' the infix-codec reopener 'by construction' both overreaches and contradicts the position's own joint-accounting concession. The codec is not a round-2 floor — round2-syntax.md explicitly parked it as admissible behind a gated A/B (follow-up line 35), and the already-ordered experiment measures 'training-distribution penalty decay with in-context examples.' Under the position's own joint spec-delta + program-delta gate, a familiarity dividend could make one extra grammar section net-negative in tokens: fewer canonical-form teaching examples needed to reach plateau, plus relief on the measured 1.35–1.48x per-program inflation. Pre-deciding a parked experiment negatively by fiat inverts the measure-then-decide discipline the position accepts for its own cap number.

The N006 alignment claim — 'the token-minimal spec and the minimal validated semantics are the same object minimized under two metrics' — is false in general. N006 prices semantic rules and rewrites at proof-grade cost; teaching tokens are dominated (per the position's own risk analysis and both rivals' example-load-bearing hypotheses) by worked examples, which add zero verification surface. Conversely a single dense rule can be few tokens and enormous proof surface. The two minimizations can diverge, so 'the budget costs the verification charter nothing' is unsupported.

The C004 citation is both stale and structurally misused. Stale: notes/card-verification-2026-07-02.jsonl shows C004 was adversarially verified on 2026-07-02 (CONFIRMED_WITH_AMENDED_SOURCES — data races prevented, general race conditions NOT), so 'flagged for adversarial verification, not workflow-confirmed' no longer describes the record. Misused: Rust's 'two predicates plus borrowing' compactness rides on Send/Sync being AUTO traits — i.e., inference — while the round-2 floors mandate zero cross-boundary inference and explicit capability/effect annotations on every signature (synthesis TYPES AND FACTS). This language's writer-facing capability surface is therefore strictly larger than Rust's, so C004 cannot underwrite 'D1 machinery fits inside a hard budget.' The uncompressed teaching cost of explicit-everywhere capabilities is exactly the unmeasured quantity the cap would bind against.

'Soft targets do not bind under adversarial feature pressure; commons problems are solved by caps' is an uncarded governance claim contradicted by in-corpus precedent: round 2's null-hypothesis-CUT was a non-numeric, burden-shifting procedural default and it demonstrably bound (ten features cut or replaced, synthesis verdict table). The position never explains why a spec-delta ledger with a CUT-unless-justified default — the same instrument that already worked — fails where only a scalar cap succeeds. Its strongest supporting observation (round-2's aggregate teaching load is unowned) argues for an owner and a default, not necessarily for a number.

Lexicographic D1-priority makes the hard cap conditionally vacuous. D1's writer-facing spec appetite is the largest unknown in the corpus: the aliasing-model round-2 rerun is unrun with 'load massively increased' and blocking obligations attached (synthesis, Round-1 verdicts affected), C004's scope was just amended, and round-3 will add escape-hatch, runtime-check-policy, FFI-attenuation, and proof-burden-on-writer text of unknown size (user-directives.md D1 open list). If D1-mandatory content approaches the cap, the 'hard' gate can bind only by evicting optimizer-necessary vocabulary (schedule layer, modes, operation tables) — colliding with D0's extreme-performance mandate — or by being ignored (the position's own risk 3). The cap is only meaningfully hard in the world where D1's teaching cost turns out small, which is the world where it was least needed.

challenges.targetPosition: Position 2 — layered kernel cap with retrieved layer specs
challenges.challenge: 'Loud by construction' is only half true, and the missing half is the dangerous one. Grammar closure catches a writer that ATTEMPTS an untaught layer construct; it cannot catch a writer that doesn't know a layer exists and emits well-typed kernel-only code with wrong semantics — kernel checked-integer mode where the wrapping mode was needed, a copy where region sharing was needed, no capability annotation where concurrency required one. That is precisely the D009 silent-degradation image the position invokes, produced by its own architecture: teaching-by-parts makes construct-selection errors MORE likely while the manifest checks only constructs used, never constructs needed. No named experiment in the position's list measures wrong-construct-selection rate under kernel-only context.

challenges.targetPosition: Position 2 — layered kernel cap with retrieved layer specs
challenges.challenge: The teaching contract — 'the writer's context must have contained the manifest's layer specs' — is unverifiable by the toolchain. Context contents are an assertion of the prompt-assembly harness, not a checkable artifact; unlike provenance-checked generation (metaprogramming mechanism 2) there is no source-of-record to diff against. The enforcement mechanism the position analogizes to contract-edit gating is actually a new trusted, unverified component (context attestation) with the same laundering shape that gating exists to prevent. The position's risk list names retrieval infrastructure but misses that the enforcement itself is trust, not check.

challenges.targetPosition: Position 2 — layered kernel cap with retrieved layer specs
challenges.challenge: The safety-rationing argument against a whole-spec cap partially attacks a strawman. N001/N004/N005 are optimizer/IR-facing UB contracts (poison ladder, range metadata, inbounds GEP) that live below the writer surface under BOTH positions' framings — Position 1's cap explicitly covers only the writable subset, with full normative semantics in unbounded toolchain artifacts (its F009 split). A writer-visible cap does not force compressing LLVM poison semantics; the writer never emits noalias metadata (facts are compiler-derived per the metaprogramming verdict). Once the strawman is removed, the two YES positions cap the same object and differ only on monolithic-vs-retrieved partitioning — a much narrower disagreement than the 'rations safety against coverage' framing claims.

challenges.targetPosition: Position 3 — no hard target; ledger + T-series lane
challenges.challenge: 'The round-2 core is already spec-minimal BY CONSTRUCTION' is an unmeasured assertion doing load-bearing work. Round-2's cut criterion was optimizer/verification necessity, not teaching cost, and the two do not coincide: the synthesis's 'Additions required because the writer is an AI' — provenance record formats, elaboration-record conventions, edit-list formats, contract-edit gating protocol, redundancy channels — is an unpriced writer-visible teaching load that soundness-minimality never audited. challenges.targetPosition: Position 3 — no hard target; ledger + T-series lane

The project decides-now-when-robust at least as often: canonical form was decided on a regex-proxy measurement the verdict itself calls inadequate; trap=abort was decided with the 'single most decision-capable missing artifact' (the three-way benchmark) still unrun; closed-world was decided with compile-loop pricing named its 'biggest unpriced cost.' The position supplies no criterion for which pattern applies to spec size, while its opponents do supply one (the relax-cheap/tighten-expensive asymmetry). Symmetrically, its own binding commitments — T-series as a blocking gate, ledger discipline holding under delivery pressure — are governance bets made from the same zero cards it prosecutes.

challenges.targetPosition: Position 3 — no hard target; ledger + T-series lane
challenges.challenge: The spec-composition claim — 'what makes real language specs enormous is exactly the material round 2 cut' — is uncarded. Likewise 'spec economy arrives by construction' (via F006/N006) conflates rule-count minimality with token minimality: restriction shrinks rules, but the likely dominant teaching tokens — worked examples and the explicit annotation vocabulary the floors mandate at every boundary — are untouched by rule-count arguments. The position's E2/E8 experiments would test this, but its Argument 4 asserts the conclusion in advance.
