missingConsiderations: The re-read channel is an unbudgeted third token stream: D2 defines spec and program budgets, but the synthesis MANDATES re-reading compiler-elaborated artifacts and interface views ('any AI workflow reading raw source without the elaborator is out of contract'). For large code units this recurring in-window load could dominate both named budgets, reordering the entire debate's stakes — no position assigns it an owner, a budget, or an experiment.

Toolchain relocation reshapes rather than removes the writer-visible contract, and nobody prices the reading side: node-path diagnostics, site-enumerated edit lists, provenance records, elaboration records, and trap reports are formats the writer must be TAUGHT TO PARSE. Both YES positions claim budget relief from relocation; no card or named experiment tests whether models consume tool-output formats more cheaply per token than language rules — the accounting identity beneath the whole displacement strategy (P1's leakage concession names the problem but no experiment isolates format-parsing cost).

The stdlib/vocabulary boundary of the cap is undefined: Result taxonomy, operation-table conventions, allocator/region APIs, and any arrays-loops schedule vocabulary are library-shaped surface the writer must be taught. For an optimizer language this plausibly exceeds core-grammar teaching cost, yet no position states whether the budget covers it.

Round-3 sequencing is named only by P2 and quantified by nobody: the proof-burden-on-writer question (D1 open list) could be the single largest writer-facing spec item — an obligation language plus discharge examples — meaning any draft-spec measurement pass run now measures the wrong language. The cap debate should state explicitly what its number means before and after round-3.

Multi-model calibration: D2 says 'models' plural; token counts are tokenizer-dependent and learning curves family-dependent. P3's T1 spans >=3 families and P1 pins tokenizer versions, but no position defines the reference model set whose knee sets a cap, or the re-certification policy when families disagree or a new generation ships.

The spec/program coupling through the shared window is treated as separable when D2 makes it joint: canonical form inflates program tokens (1.35–1.48x proxy) in every unit, and the joint optimum might BUY program relief with spec tokens (e.g., a codec grammar section). P1 concedes joint accounting then forecloses its clearest instance; P2/P3 never model the cross-term at all.

C004's amended scope creates an unpriced teaching item: the language prevents data races but NOT general race conditions. The spec must teach writers exactly where D1's guarantee ends or they will assume more safety than shipped — a mandatory kernel-grade line item touching the round-3 safety envelope that no position budgets.

Spec-of-the-spec governance: P2's single-source layered spec generation and P3's provenance ledger both require spec tooling (content-addressed, compiler-versioned, example-pairs-compiled-in-CI) that does not exist; none of the positions sequences building it relative to ratifying any cap, though every proposed enforcement mechanism depends on it.
result.sharpestTension: All three positions privately converge — the number must come from measured curves (P1 concession 1, P2 point 6, P3's T-series), and the budgeted object must be the writer-visible total contract — so the real dispute is whether the HARDNESS commitment is made before the D1 denominator is known, and that dispute sits on a calibration circularity nobody resolves. The cap is lexicographically subordinate to D1, but D1's writer-facing teaching cost is the language's largest unmeasured quantity: the aliasing-model round-2 rerun is unrun with 'load massively increased', C004's scope was just amended (data races yes, race conditions no — a boundary the spec must now teach), and round-3 will add escape-hatch, runtime-check, FFI-attenuation, and proof-burden text of unknown size. Freeze hardness now (even number-later) and the gate is calibrated against an incomplete language: under a bad draw D1+round-3 content fills any model-teachable budget and the 'hard' cap can bind only by evicting optimizer-necessary vocabulary — colliding with D0 — or by being ignored, discrediting gating (P1's own risk 3); under a good draw it binds where there was little pressure. Defer hardness until after round-3 and the documented accretion channel (the FFI periphery 'accreting into a second, evidence-free language') runs unpoliced through exactly the window when the largest writer-facing spec items land — the failure P3's ledger admittedly polices more weakly. Every position's pre-committed fallback collapses toward an opponent under stress (P2's cap-yields-to-D1 demotion becomes P3; P1's set-number-after-draft is P3's T1 with a signature; P3's threshold-triggered budget becomes P1 late), which reveals the debate as a disagreement about commitment timing under an unknown D1 denominator — and no proposed experiment (T1–T6, dose-response, ablation, census) measures the D1 line item first, because the machinery whose teaching cost dominates the calibration does not exist to be drafted, let alone measured. The operational ruling this forces: sequence the D1/round-3 spec-draft measurement as the T-series' first artifact, or any spec-size law adopted here is a number about a language that hasn't been written down.

challenges.challenge: Your headline scaling law is contradicted by your own mandatory list. You claim 'mandatory per-program tokens scale with boundary count, not interior expression volume,' but your MANDATORY tier includes explicit literal types, explicit conversions, a lifetime contract per allocation, and exhaustive match arms — all of which scale with interior expression volume. The memory-automation precedent you call 'boundary' is per-ALLOCATION (round2-memory-automation rationale (a): 'every allocation carries a writer-stated, compiler-checked storage/lifetime contract'), i.e., interior sites. The defensible claim is 'scales with intent-fact count'; the boundary-proportional claim your concession only softens ('likely undershoots') is structurally false, not approximate.

F006/SPARK is miscited as 'the shipped precedent for signature-dense/interior-light checking.' SPARK bodies are Ada: every declaration carries an explicit type; SPARK is among the most interior-annotation-dense shipped languages. What F006 actually cards (verified-findings F6) is declared boundary restrictions plus tool-discharged PROOFS — precedent for boundary contracts and restricted expressiveness, not for interior type elision. Your only shipped 'interior-light' precedent is thus F009 (facts in compiler IR), which all three positions claim equally.

Your OPTIONAL interior checksum channel violates the single-spelling floor in your regime specifically. Round2-syntax's checksum clause ('writer-stated inferable facts are legal and cross-checked') was priced under a regime where storage is the fully-elaborated form, so an optional statement never creates a second stored spelling. Under your ruling the stored source of record is the interior-light text — so the same semantics is legally storable with or without each optional annotation: multiple stored spellings per construct, and byte-level near-duplicate dedup (the consistency layer's substrate, six verdicts deep) degrades exactly at the drift-prone sites where you recommend the targeted spend. P2 conceded the analogous cost for its T3; you never price yours.

The burden-flip argument ('cost certain-and-recurring, benefit unproven') overstates the certainty asymmetry. Only the WRITE-side token saving is certain; the NET context saving depends on ambiguity-error repair-loop length, diagnostic-slice quoting volume, retrieval discipline, and re-read re-derivation error rates — all items on your own risks/EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED lists. Both sides of your ledger are empirical; D2 changes the price of one certain component, which is weaker than the presumption-flip you claim. Also, 'multipliers compose' with the 1.35–1.48x figure conflates two different axes (notation form vs annotation density) measured by a regex proxy on fragments the syntax verdict itself refuses to extrapolate.

Your 'bounded slice, so the common repair path requires no voluntary retrieval' claim assumes defect locality that the corpus explicitly denies for closed-world checking: round2-synthesis mandates cross-file provenance chains precisely because ownership violations surface far from cause. For that error class the 'slice' is a provenance chain spanning units — potentially elaborated-density context — and your unpriced diagnostic-token risk stops being pathological and becomes the D1-checker common case.

Mandating interior annotation NOW contravenes the type-inference verdict's own admissibility condition. Round2-type-inference 'What must be true' states: if interior annotations are mandated, 'the redundancy-independence experiment must have SHOWN same-pass annotations actually catch writer errors at rates justifying the measured token/cascade costs.' That experiment has not run. Your claim that your position 'requires reopening nothing' is therefore wrong: you are asking the court to convert a needs_evidence disposition into law ahead of its named gate, which is exactly the re-opening-with-cause procedure you say you avoid — and unlike P1, you cannot cite D2 as cause, because D2 raises the cost of your regime.

'The marginal spec cost of interiors-too is one sentence' is contradicted by your own tiers. T1 requires a fused atom vocabulary whose SPEC-budget table scales multiplicatively (operators × modes); T2 adds named effect-rows, named instantiations, and contract-group references — new checked core surface; T3 adds unit-head defaults, a bare-mandatory canonicality rule, new hard-error classes, and gated default edits. Your own risks list calls this 'boundary creep' that 'spends the same finite D2 spec budget my position claims to conserve.' The honest comparison is your rule-set versus the synthesis algorithm, and you concede that net is unmeasured — so the uniformity argument's headline is rhetoric, not accounting.

Your read-side argument overreads the synthesis clause and is undercut by your own citation. Synthesis line 67 makes the elaborated form the mandatory re-read ARTIFACT and puts the elaborator in-contract; it nowhere requires full-density wholesale ingestion on every read — and round2-modules, which you cite approvingly, mandates compiler-generated token-COMPACT views precisely so reads need not pay full density. If compact views work, elision's read-side penalty shrinks toward zero and 'elision saves only the write-side delta' loses its force; if they don't work, your own regime's re-reads are the most expensive on the table. Either branch weakens the one-artifact argument's quantitative half.

T3 internally contradicts your REDUNDANCY ARGUMENT. You argue full annotation 'makes that checksum channel total rather than optional,' but T3's bare-mandatory rule FORBIDS annotation at every site matching the unit-head default — eliminating the second derivation at precisely the majority of interior sites, and making the checksum spend illegal where P1's optional regime would permit it at drift-prone sites. You cannot simultaneously claim total checked redundancy as your verification advantage and ship a canonicality rule that deletes the redundant statement wherever it is cheap. Severability (dropping T3) saves consistency but surrenders most of the claimed compression.

'The writer-maximal regime pays for elaboration twice' is backwards. The synthesis's mandatory elaborated re-read artifact is explicitly conditioned on elision ('the type-inference verdict's elision option is void without it'); under full annotation the writer's source IS the elaborated artifact — one object, paid once. That identity is P2's whole one-artifact argument. Meanwhile your sole-read-back rule means YOUR regime pays elaborated density on every re-read (you concede this), so the double-payment you attribute to the rival is closer to a description of your own ledger plus a codec.

The 'four decided rulings ARE my position in miniature' claim overgeneralizes: in all four precedents (memory drops, generics expansion, error context chains, interface views) the writer-emitted text remains the stored source of record and the derived weight lives in IR/build/tooling artifacts. No round-2 ruling makes writer text TRANSIENT and stores a compiler-derived form as the semantic record — that split is a genuinely new architecture carrying the full codec/provenance-map burden, not a generalization. A006 is also miscited: Halide's schedule layer is writer-manipulable explicit program structure ('exposing optimization choices as manipulable program structure'), which is evidence for surfacing choices in writer-owned text — closer to P2's philosophy than to compiler-carried derivation.

Your bijectivity claim collapses against your own optional-checksum channel. If optional writer-stated inferable facts are legal on the write surface but storage is the elaborated single-spelling form, then strip(elaborate(x)) ≠ x for any annotated x: either optional annotations are lost on round-trip (destroying the checksum spend and the parse-elaborate-strip fixpoint), or the elaborated artifact must record which facts were writer-stated (annotation provenance = a second spelling dimension inside the 'single-spelling' store, perturbing content-addressing). Separately, your 'minimal = least text that uniquely determines semantics' is not yet well-defined: the determination requirement depends on the D1 checker's fact-demand set, and checked ownership is design_candidate_later — if the borrow/region checker needs interior witnesses (reborrows, region instantiations), your minimal form grows unpredictably and the write-grammar you claim is cheap to teach is a moving target. You also cite D009 without the unverified-card caveat the type-inference verdict itself mandates (D009/D010 are absent from the 2026-07-02 pass; P1 flags this, you do not).

unsupportedClaims: P1: 'mandatory per-program tokens scale with boundary count, not interior expression volume' — falsified by its own mandatory tier (literal types, conversions, per-allocation lifetime contracts, match arms all scale with interior volume); stated as the position's headline, only obliquely softened in a concession.

P1: 'the in-context spec cost [of the deterministic bottom-up synthesis rule] is roughly one paragraph plus examples' — an unmeasured spec-token estimate presented as fact; the same position elsewhere lists spec-rendering measurement as EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

P1: 'the common repair path requires no voluntary retrieval' — asserted as architectural fact; contradicted in the nonlocal-error case by the synthesis's own cross-file provenance-chain requirement, and its deciding experiment sits on P1's own untested list.

P1: F006/SPARK as 'shipped precedent for signature-dense/interior-light checking' — SPARK interiors are fully type-annotated Ada; the card evidences boundary contracts + proof discharge, not interior elision.

P1 and P3 (shared): 'the writer can predict what its code means by running the same rule it was taught' — a model-capability claim presented as a corollary of rule determinism; determinism of the synthesis function does not entail in-context executability by a model (both positions flag ambiguity-economics elsewhere but assert this in the core argument).

P2: 'the marginal spec cost of interiors-too is one sentence' — contradicted by its own T1–T3 machinery (fused ops×modes atom table, row aliases, named instantiations, bare-mandatory rules, default-edit gating), all of which are spec surface.

P2: 'in a read-modify-write loop the model pays elaborated-density tokens on every re-read regardless of regime' — overreads synthesis line 67 (elaborator-in-contract ≠ wholesale full-density ingestion) and is undercut by P2's own citation of round2-modules' token-compact read views.

P3: 'the writer-maximal regime pays for elaboration twice — once in generation, once in the artifact that must exist regardless' — false; the mandatory elaborated re-read artifact is conditioned on elision, and under full annotation source and artifact coincide.

P3: teaching examples 'inflate 1.35–1.48x under elaborated text on the only measured proxy' — axis transposition: the round2-syntax proxy measured S-expression vs infix NOTATION on six fragments, not elaborated-vs-minimal ANNOTATION density; no measurement of annotation-density inflation exists.

P3: 'my position is not a new bet; it is the generalization of the pattern every round-2 verdict independently converged on' — all four cited precedents keep writer text as the stored source of record; the transient-write-surface/stored-derived-artifact split has no round-2 precedent.

P3: D004 cited for keeping elaborator work off the critical path — D004 covers codegen-unit/LTO tradeoffs only (the type-inference verdict itself notes 'D004 covers only codegen-unit/LTO'); its extension to elaboration caching is uncarded.

P2: 'examples are the dominant spec cost' (asserted inside P3's and implied in P2's spec-budget arithmetic) — no measurement of spec composition (grammar vs rules vs examples) exists anywhere in the corpus; every spec-budget comparison in all three cases rests on this unstated allocation.
missingConsiderations: Round-3 safety-envelope coupling (D1 open questions explicitly delegate 'proof burden placed on AI writers,' runtime-check policy for bounds/overflow, unsafe escape-hatch form, FFI fact-attenuation): if round-3 chooses static bounds/overflow discharge, writers pay interior proof obligations (loop invariants, ghost facts) that dwarf the interior-type-restatement question all three positions litigate; if it chooses runtime checks, per-node mode vocabulary grows instead. No position conditions its per-program token policy on round-3's outcome, yet round-3 can invalidate every number in this debate.

The READ protocol is the actual unpriced budget driver in all three regimes: none specifies what a model must ingest to safely EDIT a unit (own-unit source at which density? dependency signatures? elaborated slices? compact views?). All three policies regulate write-side density while the read side — which D2's shared-context framing suggests dominates in agentic loops — is architecture sketches in P1, a conceded full-density cost in P3, and an unexamined assumption in P2.

Diagnostics double as the teaching channel: under D2 the compiler-as-teacher/progressive-disclosure question (delegated to round-4) interacts directly with this ruling — elaboration-quoting diagnostics implicitly teach the elaborated dialect (feeding P3's form-blending risk and P1's imitation risk) and may substitute for spec examples (reducing SPEC budget). No position models the diagnostic channel as a token budget line or as spec-substitute.

Familiarity asymmetry is never weighed: interior-light source is the training-distribution-shaped regime (Rust/Swift-style inference is what models already write), while uniform full annotation and fused mode-atoms are maximally alien. The round-4 'familiarity leverage vs alien canonical form' topic is entangled with this ruling; only P1 gestures at placement-transfer for signatures, and nobody notes that elision itself is the familiar regime — an uncarded but direction-setting consideration.

The unified consistency layer (six verdicts depend on it) must be re-specified per regime and its power differs: byte-identity dedup works on P2-T1/T2 stored text, requires post-expansion tree hashing under P2-T3, keys on elaborated bytes under P3, and is silently broken by P1's optional-annotation spelling variance at exactly the drift-prone sites. Only P2 partially prices this; no position compares drift-detection POWER across regimes, though drift detection is the canonical form's core justification.

Neighboring-unit context cost: D2's budget is shared with the dependency code a model reads while writing; the annotation density of OTHER units ingested as context (interfaces, callee signatures) may dominate per-task tokens, and boundary-dense signatures — mandatory under all three positions — are precisely what gets re-read most. No position analyzes whether its interior policy or the shared boundary floor drives the marginal context bill.

A cheap, decisive, tokenizer-only measurement exists today — render the fixed spec + example set and a benchmark program corpus under all three regimes with real BPE tokenizers — and all three positions list it as an experiment, yet each asks for a presumptive ruling FIRST. None proposes making the ruling conditional on this zero-model-behavior measurement, which would bound the disputed arithmetic before any behavioral experiment runs.

Vocabulary-freeze sequencing: the ownership/effect/mode/capability vocabulary this debate allocates tokens for is not stabilized (aliasing is design_candidate_later, concurrency research_needed, effects undesigned); round2-type-inference makes vocabulary stability a precondition for mass generation. All three per-program policies quantify over an unfrozen vocabulary, so any ruling here is provisional in a way none of the positions' 'presumptive default' framings acknowledges.
result.sharpestTension: The synthesis's recorded open tension — which artifact is the source of record in a repair loop — is being 'resolved' in three mutually exclusive directions on the same evidence base: P1 stores the light form and makes the elaborated view derived and write-inert; P3 stores the elaborated form and makes the writer's text transient; P2 makes source and artifact identical by paying full density. All three cite the same precedents (F009, memory-automation, modules views, the same synthesis clauses), and each position's decisive quantitative premise is the SAME uncarded quantity: where tokens are actually spent in agentic read-modify-write loops (the read:write ratio, re-derivation hallucination rate on light reads, form-blending rate on mixed reads, and diagnostic-slice volume). Because the mandatory read protocol is unspecified in every regime, 'who carries the annotation weight' is really 'who pays at read time' — and D2's token arithmetic cannot rank the three regimes until that protocol is designed and measured. The tension is sharpened by a governance fact P3 alone names: the clauses being reinterpreted ('stored artifact is fully elaborated', 'mandatory re-read artifact') are jointly owned by the syntax and type-inference verdicts, and P1's and P3's rulings each require amending one of them by fiat of this debate while claiming to preserve the floors — so whichever way the court rules, it either pre-empts the redundancy-independence experiment that round-2 named as the decider (P2's regime does so most directly, violating type-inference's own admissibility condition), or it re-opens a floor on the strength of read-side token arithmetic that no card, proxy, or measurement currently supports."
